faculty.name: Yijian (Eugene) Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on survival analysis (multi-state processes, quality-adjusted survival time, lifetime medical cost, recurrent events, quantile regression), covariate measurement error, classification and prediction, and semi- and non-parametric inferences. Applied work in cancer, HIV/AIDS, cardiovascular diseases, and kidney disease. ASA Fellow with over 80 published papers.

faculty.name: Ying Guo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Center for Biomedical Imaging Statistics (CBIS) and focuses on imaging statistics, brain network analysis, dimension reduction, blind source separation, and independent component analysis (ICA). Applies these methods to mental health, child development, and women's health research, with extensive collaboration with neurologists on depression imaging biomarkers. ASA Fellow (2018).

faculty.name: David Benkeser
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the theory and applications of machine learning in causal inference, with collaborative research on preventive vaccines, tuberculosis, and HIV prevention. Also works on survival analysis, competing risks, complex longitudinal data, and robust nonparametric statistical inference. Serves as Director of Data Science Initiatives.

faculty.name: Karen Conneely
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for genetic and epigenetic studies, focusing on genetic association studies, DNA methylation, epigenetic aging biomarkers, and integrative genomics using genomic, epigenomic, and transcriptomic data. Applies these methods to understand how environment and genetics influence health outcomes.

faculty.name: Natalie Dean
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative methods for evaluating intervention effectiveness for emerging infectious diseases, particularly vaccine trial design and observational study designs like the test-negative design. Co-directs the Emory Alliance for Vaccine Epidemiology (EAVE) and leads the CDC-funded Emory Center for Infectious Disease Modeling and Analytics Training Hub (CIDMATH).

faculty.name: Donald Lee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops nonparametric machine learning methods for survival analysis with time-dependent covariates, including the BoXHED gradient boosting framework published in Annals of Statistics. Applies these methods to healthcare settings including real-time warning indicators for stroke, early mortality prediction for cancer patients, and ICU mortality prediction.

faculty.name: Max (Siu Yin) Lau
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops innovative models and algorithms blending statistics, computational models, and ML/AI for infectious disease dynamics, phylodynamic inference, and spatiotemporal outbreak forecasting. Secondary appointments in Epidemiology and Computer Science. Actively mentors graduate students and postdocs.

faculty.name: Razieh Nabi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel causal methodologies to address unmeasured confounders, missing or censored data, measurement error, and algorithmic bias. Draws on machine learning/AI (especially graphical models) and semiparametric statistics, with applications in healthcare, social justice, and public policy. PhD from Johns Hopkins (2021) under Ilya Shpitser.

faculty.name: Chang Su
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methodology for single-cell genomics and genetics, with a focus on gene network inference and understanding the genetic basis of single-cell genomics. Applies these methods to neurodegenerative, lung, and immune diseases. Holds a secondary appointment in Human Genetics.

faculty.name: Jian Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel statistical methods for biomedical data including single-cell RNA sequencing, spatial transcriptomics, multi-omics analysis, and digital pathology. Combines statistics, machine learning, and computational biology to elucidate the structure and function of genomes, with a joint appointment in Human Genetics. PhD in Biostatistics from Penn.

faculty.name: Julia Wrobel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for functional data analysis with applications to wearable device data, spatial proteomics, multiplex single-cell imaging, and digital biomarkers (e.g., pupil light response for cannabis impairment detection). Contributes to open-source R packages including registr and mxnorm. Core faculty in Emory Center for AI Learning. PhD from Columbia (2019).

funding.note: All admitted students offered Graduate School Assistantship: full tuition, health insurance, stipend

faculty.name: Ali Arab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops hierarchical Bayesian models for spatio-temporal, spatial, and time series data with applications in ecology (migratory bird patterns under climate change), epidemiology (infectious disease spread including malaria and Lyme disease), and human rights (forecasting forced displacement using unconventional data sources). Also works on Bayesian transfer learning for data-sparse contexts.

faculty.name: Sivan Leviyang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical and statistical models of the immune response to viral infection through analysis of experimental datasets. Research spans computational transcriptomics, viral genomics, machine learning for genomics data, and single-cell RNA-seq analysis, with a focus on HIV/SIV viral evolution and CTL escape dynamics.

faculty.name: Mark J. Meyer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Functional Data Analysis, Bayesian Statistics, Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian and nonparametric methods for functional and longitudinal data analysis, including function-on-function regression, historical functional linear models, and functional inference. Focuses on making advanced statistical testing procedures accessible to non-specialists. Collaborates broadly across epidemiology, developmental psychology, neurology, global health security, environmental health, political science, and animal behavior. Affiliated with the Center for Global Health Scie

faculty.name: Mahlet G. Tadesse
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.sub_field: Bayesian Statistics, Statistical Genomics, High-dimensional Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational tools for the analysis of large-scale genomic data, including Bayesian variable selection, mixture models, MCMC methods, and integration of biological knowledge in genomic studies. Also develops predictive models for species and trait distribution patterns in highly biodiverse ecosystems. Elected Fellow of the American Statistical Association and elected member of the International Statistical Institute. Sc.D. in Biostatistics from Harvard University.

faculty.name: Maryam Yashtini
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical and computational frameworks to make AI systems more efficient, interpretable, scalable, and trustworthy. Applications include medical imaging (algorithms reducing MRI scan time from approximately 40 minutes to under 5 minutes while improving image quality), computer vision, large language models, and multimodal data integration. Ph.D. in Computational Mathematics from University of Florida, postdoc at Georgia Tech.

funding.note: Merit-based fellowships (tuition, stipend, health/dental, fees); all applicants automatically considered

faculty.name: Xihong Lin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops scalable statistical and machine learning methods for massive genomic and health data, including whole genome sequencing studies, polygenic risk prediction, causal inference, and federated learning for multi-ethnic biobanks. Also works on COVID-19 epidemic modeling and single-cell multi-omics analysis.

faculty.name: Samuel Kou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops stochastic models for single-molecule biophysics and statistical methods for Bayesian inference, including the equi-energy sampler and catalytic prior distributions. Also works on big data analytics, digital disease tracking, and Monte Carlo methods.

faculty.name: Susan Murphy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Reinforcement Learning, Causal Inference, Mobile Health
faculty.summary: Leads the Statistical Reinforcement Learning Lab, developing adaptive interventions and just-in-time adaptive interventions (JITAIs) using reinforcement learning for real-time mobile health treatment delivery. Focuses on causal inference for adaptively sampled clinical trial data in substance use, physical activity, and oral health.

faculty.name: Xiao-Li Meng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Theoretical Statistics, Bayesian Inference, Data Science
faculty.summary: Works on the theoretical foundations of statistical inference from Bayesian, Fiducial, and Frequentist perspectives, with contributions to the EM algorithm, MCMC methods, and bridge/path sampling. Founding editor-in-chief of the Harvard Data Science Review.

faculty.name: Neil Shephard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Financial Econometrics, Bayesian Nonparametrics, Causal Inference
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methodology for financial econometrics including high-frequency data analysis and simulation-based inference. Also works on defining causality for time series experiments and Bayesian nonparametric methods.

faculty.name: Kosuke Imai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Causal Inference, Computational Social Science, Political Methodology
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and machine learning algorithms for social science research, including causal inference with experimental and observational data, algorithmic redistricting analysis, data-driven policy evaluation, and fairness in algorithmic decision-making.

faculty.name: Lucas Janson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods for high-dimensional inference including model-X knockoffs for controlled variable selection, and works on reinforcement learning, uncertainty quantification for adaptively collected data, and interpretable AI. Applies these methods in genetics, political science, and microbiome research.

faculty.name: Natesh S. Pillai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computational statistics with a focus on Markov Chain Monte Carlo algorithms, reinforcement learning, and applied probability, with applications in climate science and responsible AI. Also serves as a Distinguished Engineer at LinkedIn.

faculty.name: Sham Kakade
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Foundation Models
faculty.summary: Works on the mathematical and scientific principles of large-scale machine learning, including scaling laws for foundation models, autonomous agent architectures, and full-stack training pipelines. Co-director of the Kempner Institute for the Study of Natural and Artificial Intelligence.

faculty.name: Tracy Ke
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Network Analysis, Text Analysis, High-Dimensional Statistics
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for network data analysis, text analysis and natural language processing, high-dimensional statistics, and machine learning, with applications in bioinformatics and statistical genetics.

faculty.name: Morgane Austern
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied Probability, Machine Learning Theory, High-Dimensional Statistics
faculty.summary: Develops probability tools for modern machine learning, including limit theorems for dependent and structured data, cross-validation theory, debiased machine learning, generalization bounds, and random matrix theory, with applications to stable matching problems.

faculty.name: Mark Sellke
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Probability Theory, Mathematical Physics, Theoretical Computer Science
faculty.summary: Studies computational barriers and algorithmic thresholds in random complex systems including spin glasses, high-dimensional optimization, and machine learning theory. Explores the intersection of probability, statistical physics, and theoretical computer science.

faculty.name: Pragya Sur
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: High-Dimensional Statistics, Machine Learning Theory, Causal Inference
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical frameworks for high-dimensional statistics and machine learning, including overparametrized learning, distribution shifts, high-dimensional logistic regression, and uncertainty quantification. Also works on causal inference in high dimensions.

faculty.name: Subhabrata Sen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied Probability, Spin Glasses, High-Dimensional Statistics
faculty.summary: Works on applied probability and its connections to statistics and machine learning, including sparse graph limit theory, large deviations for random graphs, spin glass theory, variational inference, and community detection on networks.

faculty.name: Joseph Blitzstein
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice
faculty.sub_field: Network Inference, Graphical Models, Statistics Education
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for network data analysis including respondent-driven sampling, link-tracing designs, and graphical models, combining probability theory and combinatorics with Monte Carlo algorithms such as sequential importance sampling and Markov chains.

faculty.name: Fadil Santosa
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods for inverse problems, wave propagation in complex media, and optimal design. Pioneered sparse L1-norm optimization for signal recovery and level-set methods for structural optimization. Recent work includes modeling direct air capture of CO2 and electromagnetics on graphene.

faculty.name: Amitabh Basu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on integer programming and mixed-integer optimization using techniques from convex geometry, geometry of numbers, and algebraic topology. Author of a forthcoming textbook on convexity in discrete and continuous optimization. NSF CAREER Award and INFORMS Egon Balas Prize recipient.

faculty.name: Carey E. Priebe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for high-dimensional and graph-structured data, including spectral graph embedding, vertex nomination, and graph matching. Applies these methods to brain connectomics, human trafficking detection, and national security. Fellow of ASA and IMS.

faculty.name: Daniel Q. Naiman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of geometry, computing, and applied statistics. Developed the theory of abstract tubes using differential geometry for improved statistical inference and computational methods. Also directs the Financial Mathematics MSE program. IMS Fellow.

faculty.name: Edward R. Scheinerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on discrete mathematics, graph theory, and combinatorics. Invented random dot product graphs, a widely used model for analyzing social and biological networks. Author of multiple textbooks including 'Mathematics: A Discrete Introduction.' Fellow of AMS.

faculty.name: Mauro Maggioni
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Develops algorithms exploiting hidden geometric structures in high-dimensional data for machine learning. Created Geometric Multi-Resolution Analysis (GMRA) for data-supported manifold approximation. Also works on learning interaction laws from particle/agent-based systems. Fellow of AMS.

faculty.name: Gregory Eyink
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads theoretical research on fluid turbulence and nonequilibrium statistical physics. Provided the first published proof of Onsager's conjecture on turbulent energy dissipation and discovered spontaneous stochasticity in turbulent flows. Fellow of American Physical Society.

faculty.name: James C. Spall
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Invented the Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation (SPSA) algorithm for efficient gradient approximation in high-dimensional optimization. Author of the most-cited textbook in stochastic optimization. Works on control, estimation, and reliability analysis. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Mark Kramer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bridges mathematical modeling, statistics, and machine learning with systems neuroscience. Develops quantitative methods to characterize neural dynamics from human brain voltage recordings, with applications to epilepsy, sleep, and memory. NSF CAREER Award recipient. Joining JHU July 2026.

faculty.name: Donniell E. Fishkind
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Works on graph theory, matrix analysis, and graph matching algorithms. Applies graph methods to topological correction in medical imaging, probabilistic path planning, and scheduling problems. Also serves as Director of Undergraduate Studies in AMS.

faculty.name: Avanti Athreya
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical inference methods for random graphs, particularly random dot product graphs and latent structure models. Works on multi-sample graph inference via omnibus embeddings, community detection, and applications to brain connectomics.

faculty.name: Nicolas Loizou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops large-scale optimization algorithms for modern machine learning, with a focus on adaptive (no-tuning-needed) learning rates, Polyak-type step sizes, and stochastic gradient methods. Also works on federated learning and algorithmic game theory. Received Johns Hopkins Catalyst Award.

faculty.name: Soledad Villar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Designs machine learning models that respect symmetries from physical laws using group theory and invariant theory. Also studies expressive power of graph neural networks and applies GNNs to combinatorial optimization. Sloan Research Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Dmitriy (Tim) Kunisky
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Explores the intersection of probability theory, mathematical statistics, and computational complexity. Studies information-computation gaps where datasets contain enough statistical signal but no efficient algorithm can detect it. Uses sum-of-squares relaxations and random matrix theory.

faculty.name: Daniel Grimmer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on extending the reach of first-order optimization theory, developing theoretical guarantees and improved algorithms for convex and structured optimization problems. PhD in Operations Research from Cornell University.

faculty.name: Elizabeth A. Stuart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops causal inference methods for evaluating public health and educational interventions, focusing on propensity score methods for non-experimental studies and assessing generalizability of randomized trials to target populations. Elected to National Academy of Medicine. Chair of Biostatistics.

faculty.name: Karen Bandeen-Roche
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Creates statistical methodology to study healthy aging and independence in older adults. Develops latent variable models and multivariate survival methods to measure frailty, resilience, and cognitive decline. Elected to National Academy of Medicine. Emeritus Chair (2008-2023).

faculty.name: Brian S. Caffo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computationally intensive statistical methods for large complex data in neuroscience, including fMRI, MRI, and PET imaging analysis. Co-created the Coursera Data Science Specialization with over 7 million enrollments. PECASE Award recipient.

faculty.name: Nilanjan Chatterjee
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for genetic epidemiology, including polygenic risk prediction, gene-environment interaction analysis, and integration of genomic data for complex disease risk modeling. COPSS Presidents' Award recipient. Joint appointment with School of Medicine.

faculty.name: Ciprian M. Crainiceanu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for high-dimensional data from wearable/implantable computing devices and neuroimaging. Co-founded the Neuroconductor R platform for reproducible medical imaging analysis. Leads the SMART research group for biological signal methodology.

faculty.name: Abhirup Datta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methods to understand how spatial, environmental, and epidemiologic factors shape population health. Works on highly multivariate geostatistical analysis for environmental exposure assessment. COPSS Emerging Leader Award recipient.

faculty.name: Marie Diener-West
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Teaches and collaborates on the design, conduct, and analysis of multicenter clinical trials, with deep commitment to statistical education and mentoring of public health graduate students. Holds the Abbey-Merrell Professorship and chairs the MPH Program.

faculty.name: Constantine Frangakis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-developed principal stratification, a foundational causal inference framework with Donald Rubin, for addressing noncompliance, missing outcomes, and surrogate endpoints in clinical trials. Applied methods to HIV needle exchange evaluation and school choice programs.

faculty.name: Frank C. Curriero
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies spatial and spatio-temporal statistical methods across public health, including geostatistical exposure assessment and models characterizing spatial variation in disease risk as a function of environmental determinants. Directs the Spatial Analysis for Public Health program.

faculty.name: Kasper D. Hansen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and open-source software for high-throughput genomics and genetics. Creator of the widely used 'minfi' Bioconductor package for DNA methylation array analysis. Joint appointment with McKusick-Nathans Institute of Genetic Medicine.

faculty.name: Hongkai Ji
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical, computational, and machine learning methods for high-throughput genomic data, with emphasis on single-cell genomics and gene regulation. Created widely used tools TSCAN and SCATE for single-cell RNA-seq and ATAC-seq data analysis.

faculty.name: Martin Lindquist
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical and statistical methods for functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) data analysis. Leads an NIH-funded national consortium developing neuroimaging-based biomarkers of pain.

faculty.name: Betsy Ogburn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops causal inference methods for complex data settings including social network data with interference between units and data with unmeasured confounding. Research recognized among the American Journal of Epidemiology's best papers.

faculty.name: Michael Rosenblum
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops innovative adaptive clinical trial designs and open-source software to efficiently determine which treatments work best for which populations. Pioneered adaptive enrichment and seamless trial designs, with extensive FDA collaboration.

faculty.name: Mei-Cheng Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops analytical methods for time-to-event data, recurrent events, and recurrent marker processes in follow-up and longitudinal studies. Contributed foundational methods for regression analysis with correlated responses alongside Scott Zeger and Kung-Yee Liang.

faculty.name: Scott L. Zeger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-developed Generalized Estimating Equations (GEE) for correlated data analysis, a landmark methodology in biostatistics. Co-directs Hopkins inHealth for precision medicine, developing Bayesian methods to use population data for improving individual clinical decisions. Member of National Academy of Medicine.

faculty.name: Stephanie Hicks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and open-source software (Bioconductor) for noisy high-dimensional biomedical data including single-cell genomics and spatial transcriptomics. Co-led landmark paper on orchestrating single-cell analysis with Bioconductor. COPSS Emerging Leader Award and ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Ni Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for high-dimensional omics data including metagenomics, genomics, and epigenomics. Known for developing MiRKAT (microbiome association test) and ConQuR (batch effect removal via conditional quantile regression). PhD Program Director.

faculty.name: Vadim Zipunnikov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced statistical methods for multimodal digital health data from wearables, smartphones, and implantables. Co-leads the Wearable and Implantable Technology group and serves as Biostatistics Director for the NIMH-funded Motor Activity Research Consortium. Collaborates with FDA on digital biomarker regulatory science.

faculty.name: Yiqun Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: AI and Statistical Methods for Biomedicine
faculty.summary: Develops AI and statistical machine learning methods for reliable biomedical research, including using large language models for evidence-based clinical Q&A and GenePT embeddings for single-cell biology. Joint appointment with Computer Science. PhD from University of Washington with Daniela Witten.

faculty.name: Leonardo Collado Torres
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops open-source R/Bioconductor tools to understand gene expression in the human brain and its role in neuropsychiatric disorders. Core developer of recount3, spatialLIBD, and derfinder packages. Investigator at the Lieber Institute for Brain Development.

faculty.name: Akihiko (Aki) Nishimura
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian methods and efficient MCMC algorithms for large-scale biomedical applications. Created the prior-preconditioned conjugate gradient method for accelerated Gibbs sampling in high-dimensional sparse regression. Laplace Award and Savage Award recipient.

funding.note: Fully funded via MacCracken (full tuition + stipend + health insurance, 4-5 yrs + 3 summers)

faculty.name: Jonathan Niles-Weed
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies statistical and computational problems arising from data with geometric structure, with much of his recent work dedicated to developing a statistical theory of optimal transport. Co-authored the monograph 'Statistical Optimal Transport' and received the 2023 IMS Tweedie New Researcher Award.

faculty.name: Carlos Fernandez-Granda
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Designs and analyzes data-science methodology with a primary focus on machine learning and its applications to medicine, climate science, and scientific imaging. Author of 'Probability and Statistics for Data Science' and Interim Director of the NYU Center for Data Science.

faculty.name: Yanjun Han
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the mathematics of data science, including statistics, learning theory, bandits, and information theory, with a recent focus on the theory of empirical Bayes methods. Holds a PhD from Stanford and completed postdocs at the Simons Institute and MIT.

faculty.name: Benjamin Peherstorfer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of computational mathematics, machine learning, and computational statistics, focusing on nonlinear model reduction, multi-fidelity methods for uncertainty quantification, and data-driven modeling of dynamical systems. Holds NSF CAREER, DoE Early Career, and AFOSR YIP awards.

faculty.name: Paul Bourgade
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests lie in probability, in particular random matrices and statistical physics, with work spanning random matrix theory, Coulomb plasmas, interacting particle systems, and eigenvector statistics. Serves as Editor for the Annals of Probability.

faculty.name: Eyal Lubetzky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the dynamics and interfaces of the Ising and Potts models, cutoff phenomena for Markov chains, and random walks and random graphs. His research bridges probability theory and combinatorics with a focus on phase transitions and mixing times.

faculty.name: Roland Bauerschmidt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory and analysis applied to statistical mechanics, including spin systems and phase transitions, renormalisation, stochastic dynamics, and random matrices. Previously Professor of Probability at the University of Cambridge.

faculty.name: Nina Holden
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory and mathematical physics, with research on Liouville quantum gravity, Schramm-Loewner evolutions, random planar maps, and statistical physics. Earned her PhD from MIT in 2018 under Scott Sheffield and was a Junior Fellow at ETH Zurich.

faculty.name: Jonathan Weare
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the design, mathematical analysis, and application of stochastic algorithms and models, drawing on probability theory, numerical analysis, and machine learning. His work is driven by collaborations in astrophysics, biophysics, computational chemistry, and climate science.

faculty.name: Gerard Ben Arous
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory including stochastic analysis, large deviations, random media, random matrices, and spin glasses, with connections to PDEs, dynamical systems, and statistical mechanics. Director of the Courant Institute and Fellow of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics.

faculty.name: Eric Vanden-Eijnden
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on mathematical and computational aspects of statistical mechanics applied across molecular dynamics, materials science, atmosphere-ocean science, fluid dynamics, and neural networks. Combines tools from probability theory, mathematical physics, numerical analysis, and optimization.

faculty.name: Sylvia Serfaty
faculty.title: Silver Professor
faculty.summary: Works on analysis, partial differential equations, and mathematical physics, with a focus on the Ginzburg-Landau model of superconductivity and the statistical mechanics of Coulomb-type systems including log gases, random matrices, and point processes. Plenary speaker at ICM 2018 and elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

faculty.name: Andrew Gordon Wilson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops a prescriptive foundation for building intelligent systems, with contributions spanning scalable Gaussian processes (creator of GPyTorch), Bayesian deep learning, model selection, and generalization theory. Discovered mode connectivity and created the SWA optimization procedure now natively included in PyTorch.

faculty.name: Joan Bruna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the mathematical foundations of machine learning, covering optimization, representation, and statistical aspects, with applications to computational science including geophysics and climate modeling. Co-founder of the Math and Data (MaD) group at NYU and affiliated with the Flatiron Institute. On sabbatical for 2025-26.

faculty.name: Jinyoung Park
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on combinatorics and graph theory, with a focus on random discrete structures and phase transitions. Co-proved the Kahn-Kalai conjecture on threshold phenomena and received the Maryam Mirzakhani New Frontiers Prize (2023) and the Levi L. Conant Prize (2025).

faculty.name: Hau-Tieng Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the MISTA Lab, establishing rigorous mathematical foundations for analyzing high-frequency physiological waveform signals (ECG, EEG, etc.) using time-frequency analysis, manifold learning, and machine learning. Holds both a PhD in Mathematics from Princeton (advised by Ingrid Daubechies) and an MD.

faculty.name: Beth Andrews
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on model fitting and prediction for nonlinear, non-Gaussian processes observed over space and time, with applications in economics, finance, geosciences, and signal processing.

faculty.name: Kaize Ding
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on data mining, machine learning, and large foundation models, with emphasis on graph neural networks, data-efficient learning (few-shot, weakly-supervised, self-supervised), and reliable AI for applications in cybersecurity, healthcare, and urban computing.

faculty.name: Larry Hedges
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on developing and applying statistical methods for social, medical, and biological sciences, with major emphasis on meta-analysis for combining results across studies, design of social experiments, and the replication crisis in science.

faculty.name: Hongmei Jiang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on design and analysis of high-throughput genomic and metagenomic data, including statistical methods for microbiome data, longitudinal trajectory subgroups for precision medicine, and multiple comparisons.

faculty.name: Wenxin Jiang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans mathematical statistics, biostatistics, data mining, Bayesian statistics, and econometrics, including work on ecological regression with partial identification and asymptotic distributions for data mining lift measures.

faculty.name: Han Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computation and data as a lens to explore science and intelligence, focusing on nonparametric structure learning and representation learning to advance both statistical machine learning and deep learning, with applications to scientific data.

faculty.name: Matey Neykov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on high-dimensional statistics and machine learning, particularly high-dimensional nonparametric estimation, variable selection, inference, and graph property testing, with applications to electronic medical records and personalized medicine.

faculty.name: Miklos Z. Racz
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of probability, statistics, computer science, and information theory, focusing on statistical inference on large random discrete structures, graph matching in correlated random graphs, community detection, and applications to DNA data storage.

faculty.name: Feng Ruan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of statistics, optimization, and machine learning, developing rigorous understanding of how to design statistical models amenable to local algorithms and developing theory for problems under resource constraints.

faculty.name: Noelle I. Samia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical models for nonstationary, serially correlated, heteroscedastic count and continuous time-series data with applications in infectious diseases, ecology, and biomedical research, and also works on multimodal large language models and AI-driven scientific discovery.

faculty.name: Thomas Severini
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on likelihood-based statistical methods such as maximum likelihood estimation, higher-order asymptotic approximations, and statistical methods for models with many parameters with applications in finance, econometrics, and sports data analysis.

faculty.name: Bradly C. Stadie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on reinforcement learning, using feedback to teach machines new behaviors, with applications to robotics, biological sciences, and large language models, including combining ChatGPT with robot control.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Tipton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for causal generalizations, improving how findings from randomized trials and meta-analyses can inform policy and practice, including methods to enhance generalizability of experiments and small-sample adjustments for meta-analysis.

faculty.name: Ji-Ping Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on statistical applications in bioinformatics and computational biology, developing machine learning methods and software tools for analyzing genomic data including nucleosome positioning, RNA-seq normalization, CRISPR-Cas9 efficiency prediction, and spatial transcriptomics.

faculty.name: Sandy Zabell
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Vince Lyzinski

faculty.name: Antoine Mellet
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Deep Ray

faculty.name: Tom Goldstein

faculty.name: Furong Huang

faculty.name: Hal Daume

faculty.name: Michelle Girvan
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Kayo Ide
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: David Jacobs
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Liza Levina
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Tailen Hsing
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Edward Ionides
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Long Nguyen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on Bayesian nonparametric methods, mixture modeling, clustering, posterior contraction rates, and high-dimensional Bayesian inference.

faculty.name: Ya'acov Ritov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on semiparametric and nonparametric statistics, survival analysis, empirical process theory, and efficient estimation in statistical models.

faculty.name: Kerby Shedden
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research encompasses computational statistics, bioinformatics, data mining, and analysis of high-dimensional and longitudinal data from biomedical studies.

faculty.name: Stilian Stoev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on extreme value theory, dependence modeling, max-stable processes, and statistical analysis of heavy-tailed and spatial extreme phenomena.

faculty.name: Ambuj Tewari
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans machine learning, online learning, reinforcement learning, high-dimensional statistics, and statistical learning theory.

faculty.name: Naisyin Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on biostatistical methods, measurement error models, nonparametric regression, mixed effects models, and longitudinal data analysis.

faculty.name: Ji Zhu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Susan A. Murphy Collegiate Professor. Research on statistical machine learning, network analysis, high-dimensional data analysis, and graphical models.

faculty.name: Moulinath Banerjee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on nonparametric inference, shape-constrained estimation, empirical process theory, and statistical methods for change-point and threshold models.

faculty.name: Johann Gagnon-Bartsch
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on causal inference methodology, experimental design with interference, and statistical methods for understanding causal effects in the presence of social network interference.

faculty.name: Ben Hansen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on causal inference, observational study design and analysis, matching methods, and sensitivity analysis for unmeasured confounding.

faculty.name: Yuekai Sun
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on machine learning, optimization methods, algorithmic fairness, and robust statistical learning theory.

faculty.name: Gongjun Xu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on latent variable models, psychometric and cognitive diagnostic modeling, high-dimensional factor analysis, and statistical methods in educational and psychological measurement.

faculty.name: Yang Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on high-dimensional statistics, graphical models, statistical network analysis, and optimization methods for structured data.

faculty.name: Colin Fogarty
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on causal inference in observational studies, randomization-based inference, sensitivity analysis for hidden bias, and design of observational studies.

faculty.name: Snigdha Panigrahi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on selective and post-selection inference, high-dimensional statistical methods, conformal inference, and distribution-free inference after model selection. Joint appointment with Biostatistics.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Regier
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on astrostatistics, probabilistic machine learning, deep generative models, and Bayesian inference for large-scale astronomical surveys.

faculty.name: Kean Ming Tan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on high-dimensional statistical methods, optimization for statistics, matrix and tensor estimation, and convex optimization approaches to structured data problems.

faculty.name: Jonathan Terhorst
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical and computational methods for population genetics, including inference of demographic history, selection, and recombination from genomic data.

faculty.name: Yixin Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on machine learning, causal inference, variational inference, and probabilistic generative modeling with an emphasis on understanding model behavior and transportability.

faculty.name: Veera Baladandayuthapani
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair and Jeremy M.G. Taylor Collegiate Professor. Research on Bayesian statistical methods for high-dimensional genomic and bioinformatic data, with applications to cancer biology and precision medicine.

faculty.name: Bhramar Mukherjee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: John D. Kalbfleisch Collegiate Professor. Research on statistical methods for gene-environment interaction studies, environmental epidemiology, and integrative analysis of high-dimensional data in population-based studies.

faculty.name: Michael R. Elliott
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on survey methodology, missing data methods, Bayesian statistical approaches, and biostatistical applications in health surveys and public health surveillance.

faculty.name: Roderick J. Little
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Richard D. Remington Distinguished University Professor. Pioneering researcher on statistical methods for missing data, multiple imputation, and Bayesian approaches to incomplete data analysis.

faculty.name: Jian Kang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Chair for Research. Research on Bayesian statistical methods for neuroimaging data analysis, spatial statistics, brain network modeling, and functional brain imaging.

faculty.name: Yi Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: M. Anthony Schork Collegiate Professor. Research on survival analysis, semicompeting risks models, longitudinal data methods, and clinical trial design and analysis.

faculty.name: Peter X. K. Song
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on longitudinal data analysis, generalized estimating equations, composite likelihood methods, and semiparametric inference for clustered and correlated data.

faculty.name: Zhenke Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on Bayesian latent variable models, methods for electronic health records and biomedical data integration, with applications in precision medicine and health disparities research.

faculty.name: Xu Shi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on causal inference with incomplete and surrogate outcomes, semi-supervised and transfer learning methods for electronic health records data.

faculty.name: Walter Dempsey
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on causal inference and experimental design for mobile health and intensive longitudinal data from wearables and smartphones.

faculty.name: Irina Gaynanova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on high-dimensional statistical methods for multi-omics data integration, covariance estimation, and sparse multivariate methods with biomedical applications.

faculty.name: Alexander Tsodikov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on survival analysis, cure rate models, competing risks, semiparametric methods, and statistical modeling for cancer outcomes and screening.

faculty.name: Xiaoquan (William) Wen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical genetics methods including fine-mapping, colocalization analysis, and Bayesian methods for integrating GWAS with functional genomic data.

faculty.name: Donglin Zeng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on semiparametric statistical methods, machine learning and deep learning for biomedical data, survival analysis, and dynamic treatment regimes for precision medicine.

faculty.name: Hui Jiang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computational statistics, high-dimensional inference, post-selection inference methods, and resampling-based approaches for complex datasets.

faculty.name: Sebastian Zollner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical methods for genetic association studies, population genetics inference, and genomic data analysis for understanding complex disease genetics.

faculty.name: Menggang Yu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on survival analysis, competing risks, causal inference in observational health data, and risk prediction models with applications to cancer outcomes and health services research.

faculty.name: Mousumi Banerjee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Anant M. Kshirsagar Collegiate Research Professor. Research on survival analysis, clinical trial design, competing risks, and cancer biostatistics with applications to oncology research.

faculty.name: Susan Murray
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on survival analysis methodology, competing risks, pseudo-observation approaches, and clinical trial design with applications to organ transplantation and cancer.

faculty.name: Trivellore E. Raghunathan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical methods for missing data, multiple imputation techniques, survey sampling, and applications to health disparities and public health research.

faculty.name: Lu Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical methods for dynamic treatment regimes and precision health, sequential decision making, and causal inference for longitudinal observational data.

faculty.name: Kelley M. Kidwell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Interim Associate Dean for Faculty Affairs. Research on design and analysis of sequential multiple assignment randomized trials (SMART), dynamic treatment regimes, and precision medicine clinical trials.

faculty.name: Thomas M. Braun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on innovative clinical trial designs including dose-finding, phase I/II designs, Bayesian adaptive designs in oncology and other therapeutic areas.

faculty.name: Hyun Min Kang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on statistical and computational methods for genetic and genomic studies, including rare variant analysis, sequencing data analysis, and genome-wide association studies.

faculty.name: Jean Morrison
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: John G. Searle Assistant Professor. Research on Bayesian statistical methods for genetic fine-mapping, colocalization, and integrating genetic data across diverse populations and ancestries.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Shankar Bhamidi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches stochastic processes, random networks including dynamics on network models and random graphs, seeking unifying mathematical principles to solve problems arising from applied science where probability can offer meaningful insight.

faculty.name: Andrew Nobel
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research driven by statistical analysis of data from genomics and biomedicine, spanning statistical applications, methodology, and theory in machine learning, computational genomics, and data mining, including expression quantitative trait loci analysis and inference for dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Mariana Olvera-Cravioto
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in applied probability focusing on stability of strongly connected components in directed random graphs under perturbations, and developing Laplace inversion methods for solving distributional fixed-point equations that arise across operations research, computer science, and statistical physics.

faculty.name: Gabor Pataki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies convex and integer programming with particular emphasis on semidefinite programming, investigating the geometry of linear images of the cone of symmetric positive semidefinite matrices to understand duality behaviors and develop preprocessing algorithms.

faculty.name: Richard Smith
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on extreme value theory, environmental statistics, and spatial statistics, with applications in climate change detection and attribution of human influence on climate, air pollution health effects, and dementia/Alzheimer's disease using hierarchical Bayesian methods.

faculty.name: Nilay Tanık Argon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on analysis, design, and control of stochastic systems applied to manufacturing, service, and healthcare operations, using Markov decision processes, sample-path analysis, approximation methods, and simulation with a data-oriented approach in recent healthcare projects.

faculty.name: Amarjit Budhiraja
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans probability, stochastic analysis, and large deviations, including stochastic control, stochastic differential games, stochastic networks, stochastic partial differential equations, random graphs, stochastic numerics, and nonlinear filtering. Co-author of 'Analysis and Approximation of Rare Events'.

faculty.name: Jan Hannig
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans data science, theoretical statistics, and probability, with primary focus on generalized fiducial inference, data integration methodology, and applications of analytical probability to engineering, finance, genomics, and forensic science.

faculty.name: J. S. (Steve) Marron
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers object oriented data analysis, connecting statistics with non-standard mathematics including geometry for shape analysis and topological data analysis. Collaborates with genomicists on high-dimensional cancer data, computer scientists on shape in image analysis, and physicians on data-centric arthritis research.

faculty.name: Vladas Pipiras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on high-dimensional time series and spatial modeling with sparse regularization, extremal behavior of dynamical systems (extreme ship motions with US Navy), sampling and streaming algorithms for big network data, and scaling/self-similar phenomena.

faculty.name: Quoc Tran-Dinh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on theory, algorithms, and applications of numerical optimization for convex and non-convex continuous optimization, including decomposition methods and matrix optimization, applied to signal/image processing, statistics, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Kai Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on post-selection inference, high-dimensional inference, and nonparametric inference, with representative work including the BET on Independence test and methods for valid post-selection inference.

faculty.name: Serhan Ziya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on stochastic modeling applied to healthcare operations, service operations, queueing design and control, and revenue management, using techniques from stochastic modeling and operations research to tackle application-driven problems, particularly in healthcare.

faculty.name: Sayan Banerjee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory focusing on connections between probabilistic couplings and geometry, interacting particle systems, random walks in random environments, and random matrices, with recent emphasis on coupling, geometry and ergodicity of diffusion processes and dynamic random networks.

faculty.name: Yao Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops algorithms for challenges in the machine learning pipeline, investigating statistical and computational dimensions with emphasis on model security, large language models, and computational pathology applications.

faculty.name: Zhengwu Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops effective statistical and machine learning methods for high-dimensional 'objects' with low-dimensional underlying structures, with recent work centered on extracting knowledge from large neuroimaging datasets using novel machine learning techniques.

faculty.name: Nicolas Fraiman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probability and statistics focusing on random structures, combinatorial statistics, and randomized algorithms, with applications to random networks, evolutionary dynamics, and Markov chains.

faculty.name: Chuanshu Ji
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in financial econometrics and probability approximations in asset pricing, focusing on calibration of stochastic volatility models, market microstructure models, high-frequency trading, and algorithmic trading.

faculty.name: Guanting Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of sequential decision making, stochastic modeling, and optimization, focusing on how agents interacting with unknown environments can improve decisions over time, with applications in sustainability and finance.

faculty.name: Zoe Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory focusing on interacting particle systems, random dynamic graphs, and mixing time of Markov chains on random graphs, with particular emphasis on the contact process within spatial stochastic models.

faculty.name: Daniel Kessler
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical analysis of networks, post-selective inference, and high-dimensional statistics, with applications in neuroimaging and cognitive neuroscience.

faculty.name: Patrick Lopatto
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on problems arising from causal inference, high-dimensional statistics, and random matrix theory, with emphasis on making statistics engaging for students.

faculty.name: Fan Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Models human-AI interactions to understand their societal impacts and design interventions with theoretical guarantees, applying tools from algorithmic game theory, learning theory, and optimization to understand multi-party dynamics in online recommendation platforms and social media.

faculty.name: Hang Zhou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on functional and longitudinal data analysis, statistical inference for object-valued data, learning theory, and random matrix theory, developing theoretically grounded statistical models for complex structured data across scientific applications.

faculty.name: Mo Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in the STOR department. Personal webpage available for research details.

faculty.name: Ali Mohammad Nezhad
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on computational complexity in optimization and its algorithmic implications for data science and engineering, with recent focus on stability, complexity, and computation in semidefinite and polynomial optimization using algebraic geometry, topology, and singularity theory.

faculty.name: Michael O'Neill
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on continuous nonlinear optimization and stochastic optimization algorithms, with emphasis on worst-case complexity theory and developing algorithms for large-scale machine learning and data science applications.

faculty.name: Ben Seeger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on stochastic analysis and the effects that noise has on models from physical or social sciences, particularly theory and applications of mean field games and interacting agent models.

faculty.name: Chudi Zhong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in STOR with joint appointment in the School of Data Science and Society. Personal website available for detailed research interests.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Michael Hudgens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on survival analysis, causal inference, and infectious disease epidemiology, with over 300 peer-reviewed papers on statistical methods including work on HIV, interference in treatment effects, and efficient nonparametric estimation.

faculty.name: Jianwen Cai
faculty.title: Cary C. Boshamer Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Has over 20 years of experience in interdisciplinary collaborative research, developing statistical methods for epidemiologic and observational studies spanning survival analysis, clinical trial design, correlated response analysis, and disease areas including cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and obesity.

faculty.name: Michael R. Kosorok
faculty.title: W.R. Kenan, Jr. Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans biostatistics, data science, machine learning and precision medicine, including work on dynamic treatment regimes and novel statistical methods for cancer clinical trials. Authored a major text on theoretical foundations of biostatistics and applies methods to cancer and cystic fibrosis research.

faculty.name: Joseph G. Ibrahim
faculty.title: Alumni Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian inference, missing data problems, medical imaging analysis, and genomics. Leads the Laboratory for Innovative Clinical Trials and serves as PI on multiple grants developing statistical methodology for clinical trials and cancer/genomics research, with over 360 publications.

faculty.name: Hongtu Zhu
faculty.title: Kenan Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical learning, medical image analysis, precision medicine, biostatistics, artificial intelligence, and big data analytics, with over 345 published papers and work on imaging genomics, brain disorders, and knowledge graphs.

faculty.name: Danyu Lin
faculty.title: Dennis Gillings Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on survival analysis, statistical genetics, and design of medical and public health studies, with applied work spanning cancer, infectious diseases, and COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness. Several of his methods have been incorporated into commercial software packages.

faculty.name: Anastasia Ivanova
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Has over 20 years of experience in clinical trial design, focusing on adaptive designs, dose-finding methods, and enrichment designs. Serves as PI for a precision medicine trial for severe asthma and co-PI for a back pain research data center.

faculty.name: Yun Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and computational tools for analyzing genetic data related to complex diseases, spanning genotype imputation, rare variant analysis, sequencing data methods, and detecting chromosome interactions from Hi-C data to help annotate GWAS findings.

faculty.name: Fei Zou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methodologies for genetic data analysis, focusing on genetic mapping approaches and developing machine learning algorithms such as deep learning for big EHR data and high-dimensional genetic and genomic data.

faculty.name: Haibo Zhou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental statistics, outcome-dependent sampling designs, survival analysis, measurement error, and reproductive epidemiology, developing innovative and cost-effective sampling designs to help researchers collect more informative samples at a fixed budget.

faculty.name: David Couper
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on coordinating large-scale clinical trials and observational studies, particularly in cardiovascular disease (ARIC study), cognitive aging (ACHIEVE trial), and COPD (SPIROMICS), with interests in data quality and epidemiological methods.

faculty.name: Bethany Hedt-Gauthier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on strengthening health systems in resource-limited settings, particularly Rwanda, through implementation science, health equity, and AI-enabled digital tools to support community health worker-led interventions for safe peripartum care in rural areas.

faculty.name: Xianming Tan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical methods for studying population heterogeneity, developing advanced approaches for clinical trials and public health including Bayesian methods, mixture models, and AI integration for applications like automated systematic reviews.

faculty.name: Tanya P. Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for high-dimensional variable selection, longitudinal data analysis, and prediction models, with applications to neurodegenerative diseases such as Huntington's disease and the gut microbiome, working with large, highly correlated data structures.

faculty.name: Michael Love
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for analyzing high-throughput sequencing assays, creating open-source software for genomic data analysis. Creator of the widely-used DESeq2 package for differential gene expression analysis from RNA-seq data.

faculty.name: Naim Rashid
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on precision medicine and cancer genomics, multi-study replicability, and missing data problems in deep learning, addressing problems facing basic science, translational, and clinical researchers in cancer. Also serves as a trial statistician on active cancer clinical trial protocols.

faculty.name: Di Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel statistical methods for analyzing biomedical and genomics data, focusing on mediator analysis-based data integration tools to infer likely causal relations across multiple levels of genomic, microbiome, and metabolites data, with new projects incorporating electronic medical records.

faculty.name: Annie Green Howard
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in using longitudinal models and latent variables to study the integration of biology, behavior, and environment related to obesity and cardiometabolic disease, with applications in large-scale cohort studies including CARDIA and Add Health.

faculty.name: Quefeng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Creates new statistical tools for analyzing complex data from biomedical studies, including methods for high-dimensional data classification, variable selection, robust estimation, and data integration to improve reproducibility across datasets like GEO and TCGA.

faculty.name: Didong Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for complex, high-dimensional data, with areas including manifold learning, nonparametric Bayesian inference, information geometry, and spatial statistics, applied to electronic health records, genetics, and single-cell RNA sequencing.

faculty.name: Lina Montoya
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Methodological research focuses on causal inference and precision medicine, exploring treatment effect heterogeneity and optimal dynamic treatment regimes, with applications in the US criminal legal system and HIV care adherence in sub-Saharan Africa.

faculty.name: Ivana Malenica
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of causal inference, non- and semi-parametric statistics, and machine learning, focusing on challenges in longitudinal and structured dependent settings including adaptive experiments, online and reinforcement learning, and personalized health applications.

faculty.name: Xihao Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methods for scalable and integrative analysis of large whole-genome/exome sequencing and multi-omics data, along with meta-analysis across diverse populations, pleiotropic genetic effect identification, and rare disease clinical trial methodology.

faculty.name: Beibo Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in clinical trials and observational studies, with research centering on improving subgroup analysis — identifying optimal treatment subgroups with accurate inferences across multiple outcomes and diverse data types. Serves as co-investigator on HCHS/SOL, CAMEO, and BACPAC studies.

faculty.name: Daiwei (David) Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel machine learning frameworks for analyzing high-dimensional, multi-modal, multi-scale data from spatial omics, computational pathology, and medical imaging, aiming to apply AI to biomedical and healthcare challenges.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

requirements.admission_model: Applicants list up to 5 faculty of interest in application

faculty.name: David Adalsteinsson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts research spanning biology, interface problems, spatial modeling, algorithm development, and scientific computing visualization tools, developing mathematical and computational methods for spatial modeling in biology with practical applications through his company Visual Data Tools.

faculty.name: Jaime Arguello
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in aggregated search (integrating results from multiple sources) and search assistance (helping users with complex search tasks), along with information retrieval, data mining, and machine learning. Recipient of an NSF CAREER Award and directs the Interactive Information Systems Lab.

faculty.name: Amina Abdu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines AI governance, focusing on how quantification, law, institutions, and values shape the algorithmic implementation of public policy. Collaborates with policy organizations such as the Federation of American Scientists to translate academic findings into policy recommendations.

faculty.name: Iain Carmichael
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning algorithms for complex data such as networks, multi-modal data integration, and high-resolution medical imaging, with a focus on computational pathology.

faculty.name: Can Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research includes control theory, network science, machine learning, deep learning, tensor algebra, numerical analysis, and computational biology, with a specific focus on using hypergraph learning to interpret biological networks.

faculty.name: Anita Crescenzi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies time constraints and time pressure in exploratory information-seeking, information search for decision-making, metacognitive regulation, and search system design for learning and problem-solving, along with FAIR data practices.

faculty.name: Snehalkumar 'Neil' Gaikwad
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in responsible AI and policy, examining ethical issues surrounding equity, access, and discrimination in algorithmic system design. Founding Director of the Society-Centered AI Lab with appointments in Data Science and Computer Science.

faculty.name: Hsun-Ta Hsu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes the use of artificial intelligence to address societal disparities and improve systems, with research at the intersection of AI and social welfare.

faculty.name: Harlin Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on learning from high-dimensional data on graphs, networks, or low-dimensional subspaces, with applications in health care and social science. Holds affiliations in Math, Computer Science, and Computational Medicine.

faculty.name: Youzuo Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research areas include physics-informed machine learning, deep learning, and computational methods applied to computational imaging, signal/image analysis, subsurface imaging, medical imaging, cancer detection, and earthquake detection.

faculty.name: Yifei Lou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of computational mathematics and data sciences, focusing on signal/image recovery from limited measurements constrained by technical or economic limits.

faculty.name: Alex McAvoy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: An applied mathematician working in evolutionary dynamics, game theory, multi-agent learning, and networks, studying how behaviors spread in populations via heterogeneous social networks.

faculty.name: Santiago Olivella
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Bayesian Methods and Political Data Science
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian probabilistic models and machine learning techniques to study electoral and legislative politics, bridging political science with data science methodology.

faculty.name: Huaxiu Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches building reliable and responsible foundation models from both theoretical and applied perspectives, working on making large-scale AI systems more trustworthy.

faculty.name: Jack Snoeyink
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor with joint appointment in Computer Science and SDSS, with expertise in computational geometry and algorithms applied to data science problems.

faculty.name: Justin Sola
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor with joint appointment in Sociology and SDSS, working at the intersection of computational methods and social science research.

faculty.name: Ran Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor joining SDSS in July 2025 from a postdoctoral position at University of Washington. Further research details available via SDSS directory.

faculty.name: Weitong Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in SDSS who earned a PhD from UCLA. Further research details available via the SDSS directory.

faculty.name: Tarek Zikry
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Faculty joining SDSS in July 2025 from a postdoctoral position at Columbia University's Zuckerman Institute, with membership in the Medical School's Bioinformatics and Computational Biology T32 training program.

faculty.name: Keriayn Smith
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Academic scientist focused on RNA structure and molecular interactions; also founded a nonprofit supporting underrepresented STEM students. Research bridges molecular biology with data science approaches.

programs.program_name: Applied and Computational Mathematics and Statistics PhD

funding.note: Full-tuition scholarship + stipend typical ($39k-44k for 2026-27 per Grad School)

faculty.name: Martina Bukač
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops numerical methods for fluid-structure interaction problems, particularly modeling blood flow and vessel dynamics to understand cardiovascular disease. Works at the intersection of numerical analysis, computational mathematics, and biomechanics.

faculty.name: Stefano Castruccio
faculty.title: Notre Dame Collegiate Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops spatio-temporal statistical models and physics-informed machine learning methods for environmental and climate applications, including renewable energy assessment and air pollution mortality. Fellow of ASA and elected member of ISI. Recipient of 2024 Gordon Bell Prize.

faculty.name: Guosheng Fu
faculty.title: Robert and Sara Lumpkins Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops high-order numerical methods including hybridizable discontinuous Galerkin (HDG) methods for computational fluid dynamics, computational mechanics, and optimal transport problems. Contributes to open-source finite element libraries NGSolve and MFEM.

faculty.name: Kelsey Gasior
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops biologically grounded multiscale models using ODEs, PDEs, and phase field methods to study intracellular phase separation and epithelial mesenchymal transition, bridging applied mathematics and cell biology.

faculty.name: Yuefeng Han
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for complex high-dimensional data structures including tensors and matrices, along with stochastic optimization techniques and robust approaches to time series analysis and classification problems.

faculty.name: Jonathan Hauenstein
faculty.title: Robert and Sara Lumpkins Collegiate Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Develops numerical methods for solving systems of nonlinear equations, implemented in the Bertini software package and applied across science and engineering. Fellow of the American Mathematical Society, Sloan Research Fellow.

faculty.name: Bei Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Partial Differential Equations and Mathematical Biology
faculty.summary: Studies free boundary problems in mathematical biology modeled by PDEs, with rigorous analysis of qualitative system behavior using elliptic/parabolic estimates, bifurcation analysis, and numerical simulations. Past work spans thermal runaway, mathematical finance, and optimal control.

faculty.name: Soham Jana
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods in statistical machine learning including robust clustering for mixture models, high-dimensional and nonparametric statistics, empirical Bayes estimation, and causal inference. PhD from Yale (2022), postdoc at Princeton before joining Notre Dame in 2024.

faculty.name: Jun Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods and algorithms for big datasets that challenge traditional statistical approaches. PhD from Stanford under Robert Tibshirani, focusing on methodology at the intersection of statistics and computation.

faculty.name: Alan Lindsay
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational and analytical techniques to study PDEs emerging from physical and biological systems, with applications in Micro Electro Mechanical Systems, mathematical ecology, and imaging/inverse problems.

faculty.name: Fang Liu
faculty.title: Notre Dame Collegiate Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.sub_field: Data Privacy and Statistical Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Develops modern approaches for data privacy protection including differential privacy, alongside research in statistical machine learning, Bayesian methods, and missing data analysis. Fellow of ASA and elected member of ISI.

faculty.name: Ondrej Maxian
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Builds computational models to study how cells tune their internal components for essential tasks like migration, division, and polarization, with emphasis on efficient numerical methods for simulating actin filaments and accessory proteins.

faculty.name: Robert Rosenbaum
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Develops computational and mathematical models of neural circuits in the brain, studying excitatory-inhibitory balance in cortical circuits. Recent work explores the interface of neuroscience and AI.

faculty.name: Daniele Schiavazzi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on stochastic analysis, multi-resolution approximation, numerical modeling, and finite element analysis, with applications to adaptive MCMC estimation and computational models for clinical decision-making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Tiffany Tang
faculty.title: Clare Boothe Luce Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Creates interpretable statistical machine learning methods to extract actionable insights from real-world medical data, with focus on transparent AI in healthcare. Builds open-source tools to promote dependable data science practices. PhD from UC Berkeley (2023).

faculty.name: Charles Wampler
faculty.title: Huisking Foundation, Inc. Collegiate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in numerical algebraic geometry, developing algorithms for solving systems of nonlinear equations. Co-author of the seminal paper in the field. Applied work spans robotics for automotive manufacturing, EV battery modeling, and NASA's Robonaut 2. Member of National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Zhiliang Xu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops high-order accurate numerical schemes for PDEs arising in classical fluid dynamics and complex fluids problems, with applications in computational physics and biology.

faculty.name: Xiufan Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods for high-dimensional statistical inference including power-enhanced hypothesis testing, causal discovery, and factor models, with applications in genomics. Elected member of the International Statistical Institute. PhD from Penn State (2021).

faculty.name: Zecheng Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on mathematical analysis and computational applications of operator learning for scientific problems including PDE solving and model reduction, with focus on foundation models for science. Research funded by DOE AI for Science.

faculty.name: Yongtao Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops high-order accuracy numerical methods including WENO schemes, fast sweeping methods, and discontinuous Galerkin finite element methods for PDEs, with applications in morphogenesis, tissue patterning, and shock wave interactions.

faculty.name: Changbo Zhu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methodology for complex non-Euclidean data including functional data analysis, distributional data analysis, and inference on metric spaces.
