Published in Biometrika and JRSSB on testing independence for sparse functional data and geodesic optimal transport regression.

faculty.name: Jeffrey S. Morris
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops hybrid ML-AI/statistical tools for extracting knowledge from large-scale biomedical data, including flexible regression methods for complex biomedical objects (functions, images, manifolds) and multi-platform data integration. Extensive applied work in cancer molecular subtyping. ASA Fellow, IMS Fellow.

faculty.name: Russell T. Shinohara
faculty.title: Professor and Deputy Director of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative biomedical imaging methods to evaluate structural and functional brain changes across the lifespan and in neurological, psychiatric, and developmental disorders.

faculty.name: Hongzhe Li
faculty.title: Perelman Professor of Biostatistics, Epidemiology and Informatics; Vice Chair of Research Integration
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Genomics and Big Data in Health
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for high-throughput genomic data including GWAS, genetic pathway analysis, and microbiome/metagenomics. Directs the Center of Statistics in Big Data. Actively recruits PhD students.

faculty.name: Nandita Mitra
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops doubly robust methods for cost-effectiveness estimation and instrumental variable approaches for censored outcomes. Co-Director of the Center for Causal Inference. Editor-in-Chief of Observational Studies. ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Jinbo Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.sub_field: Electronic Health Records and Risk Prediction
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for risk prediction using electronic health record data, addressing phenotyping inaccuracies, algorithm fairness, and performance monitoring. Director of the Statistical Center for Translational Research in Medicine. Associate Director of Penn Medicine Biobank.

faculty.name: Yong Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops methods for evidence synthesis from multiple data sources including meta-analysis and distributed algorithms for comparative effectiveness research. Founder and Director of the Center for Health AI and Synthesis of Evidence (CHASE). ASA Fellow. Over 200 peer-reviewed publications.

faculty.name: Warren B. Bilker
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops methodological approaches for pharmacoepidemiology and survival analysis, with extensive applied work in schizophrenia research linking brain imaging to clinical course. Leads the biostatistics core of Penn's Schizophrenia Center.

faculty.name: Douglas E. Schaubel
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops survival analysis methods for evaluating time-dependent treatments, with applications in end-stage renal and liver disease and organ transplantation. ASA Fellow. Associate editor at Biometrics and Statistics in Medicine.

faculty.name: Qi Long
faculty.title: Professor and Vice Chair of Faculty Professional Development
faculty.summary: Develops robust statistical and machine learning methods for complex multimodal health data (omics, EHR, imaging, mHealth). Leads coordinating centers for NCI and NHLBI multi-site clinical studies. Elected fellow of AAAS, AIMBE, ASA, IMS, ISI, and AMIA.

faculty.name: Mingyao Li
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for single-cell transcriptomics to study cellular heterogeneity in human tissues, with applications in Alzheimer's, heart disease, and gene therapy. Director of Biostatistics for Penn's Gene Therapy Program.

faculty.name: Brielin Chase Brown
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for genetic and genomic data analysis.

faculty.name: Iain Mathieson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Uses statistical and computational methods to study human evolution, population history, and natural selection through analysis of ancient and modern DNA.

faculty.name: Knashawn H. Morales
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.sub_field: Clinical Trials and Mental Health Statistics
faculty.summary: Develops and applies biostatistical methods for behavioral intervention studies and mental health research, with focus on longitudinal data analysis and clinical trial design.

faculty.name: Mary E. Putt
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops biostatistical methods for clinical studies, diagnostic testing evaluation, and survival analysis with biomedical applications.

faculty.name: Sharon Xiangwen Xie
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for survival analysis and longitudinal data with applications in Alzheimer's disease and neurodegenerative disorders.

faculty.name: Yimei Li
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.sub_field: Clinical Trial Design and Pediatric Oncology
faculty.summary: Develops innovative clinical trial designs including Bayesian adaptive methods, with applications in pediatric oncology and cancer research.

faculty.name: Wei-Ting Hwang
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops and applies biostatistical methods in cancer clinical research, including imaging biomarker evaluation and clinical trial methodology.

faculty.name: Justine Shults
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.sub_field: Longitudinal Data and Correlated Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for longitudinal and correlated data analysis, including generalized estimating equation approaches, with clinical and epidemiological applications.

faculty.name: Kai Tan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and systems biology approaches to study gene regulatory networks and cellular systems, with applications in cancer biology.

faculty.name: Yun Li
faculty.title: Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for genetic and genomic studies, focusing on genome-wide association studies and functional genomics.

faculty.name: Dokyoon Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biostatistics
faculty.sub_field: Genomic Data Science and Precision Medicine
faculty.summary: Develops computational and data science methods for integrating genomic data to advance precision medicine.

requirements.admission_model: Department admits via Wharton Doctoral Programs

funding.note: Full fellowship covering tuition plus living stipend for all doctoral students

faculty.name: Bhaswar B. Bhattacharya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical theory and methods at the intersection of nonparametric statistics, distribution-free inference, and network analysis. Works on subgraph counting, two-sample tests for random graphs, optimal transport-based inference, and discrete geometry. PhD from Stanford (2016).

faculty.name: Enric Boix
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Theory of AI and Deep Learning
faculty.summary: Studies theoretical foundations of AI including model distillation, reasoning models, steering and monitoring, training dynamics, and adversarial examples. Published work on universal steering of AI models (Science 2026) and the features at convergence theorem for neural networks.

faculty.name: Tony Cai
faculty.title: Daniel H. Silberberg Professor
faculty.sub_field: High-Dimensional Statistics and Statistical Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Develops fundamental statistical theory and methodology for high-dimensional problems, including heteroskedastic PCA, sparse inference, distributed estimation, and multiple testing. Recipient of COPSS Presidents' Award (2008). Former IMS President (2023-2025).

faculty.name: Sky Cao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on probability theory, stochastic geometric analysis, Yang-Mills theory, and Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, bridging pure mathematical probability with statistical computation.

faculty.name: Yuxin Chen
faculty.title: Gilbert Helman Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Machine Learning and Reinforcement Learning
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical foundations for reinforcement learning (offline RL, multi-agent RL), nonconvex optimization, and high-dimensional statistics. Focuses on statistical optimality and sample complexity. PhD from Stanford EE (2015), postdoc with Emmanuel Candès.

faculty.name: Damek Davis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Optimization for Statistics and Data Science
faculty.summary: Develops optimization algorithms for modern data science with emphasis on nonsmooth optimization, stochastic approximation, and convergence guarantees. Works on variance reduction, mixture model clustering, and computational microscopy.

faculty.name: Edgar Dobriban
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on uncertainty quantification (conformal prediction, calibration), AI safety and robustness (jailbreaking, watermarking LLMs), high-dimensional asymptotic statistics, and fairness in ML. PhD from Stanford (2017), T.W. Anderson Dissertation Award.

faculty.name: Giles Hooker
faculty.title: Harry J. Loman Professor and Department Chair
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning and Functional Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Develops methods for interpretable machine learning, functional data analysis, and statistical ecology. Studies stability and explainability of ML models, with applications in ecology and population dynamics.

faculty.name: Jiaoyang Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Random Matrix Theory and High-Dimensional Probability
faculty.summary: Works on random matrix theory, random graphs, interacting particle systems, and their connections to deep learning optimization and Bayesian posterior sampling. Sloan Research Fellow (2024), NSF CAREER Award (2024).

faculty.name: Shane T. Jensen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian multilevel models for spatial statistics and urban analytics, studying how urban greening affects crime and property. Also works on sports analytics including hockey and baseball player evaluation. Savage Award recipient (2005).

faculty.name: Ying Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops distribution-free inference methods including conformal prediction, with extensions to graph neural networks and foundation models. Works on policy learning, causal effect generalization, and selective inference. Ingram Olkin Award (Stanford, 2024).

faculty.name: Eugene Katsevich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for high-dimensional variable selection, multiple testing with FDR control, and knockoff-based inference, with applications to genetics and genomics. PhD from Stanford (2019).

faculty.name: Mark G. Low
faculty.title: Walter C. Bladstrom Professor
faculty.summary: Works on statistical decision theory, nonparametric function estimation, and statistical inference, with focus on minimax optimality, adaptive estimation, and confidence bands under shape constraints. IMS Fellow (2004).

faculty.name: Zhimei Ren
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Distribution-Free Inference and Data-Driven Decision Making
faculty.summary: Develops distribution-free inference methods with provable guarantees for prediction, causal inference, and data-driven decision making. Works on conformalized survival analysis and adaptive discovery. PhD from Stanford (2021) under Emmanuel Candès.

faculty.name: Dylan S. Small
faculty.title: Universal Furniture Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for causal inference from observational studies, including sensitivity analysis, matched studies, and instrumental variable models. Applied collaborations in Alzheimer's disease, anesthesia, and public health. IMS Medallion Lecturer (2022).

faculty.name: Weijie Su
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods in differential privacy (Gaussian DP), high-dimensional statistics (SLOPE), optimization for deep learning, and large-scale multiple testing. Recipient of COPSS Presidents' Award (2026), Sloan Research Fellowship (2020). PhD from Stanford (2016).

faculty.name: Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen
faculty.title: University Professor
faculty.summary: Develops semiparametric efficiency theory with applications to causal inference, missing data problems, and statistical genetics. Recipient of the Rousseeuw Prize for Statistics (2022) and David Cox Medal (2025).

faculty.name: Yuting Wei
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Machine Learning and Reinforcement Learning
faculty.summary: Develops statistical foundations for reinforcement learning (policy evaluation, multi-agent RL) and high-dimensional statistics (Lasso, AMP). NSF CAREER Award (2022), ASA Gottfried E. Noether Award (2025). PhD from UC Berkeley (2018) under Martin Wainwright.

faculty.name: Abraham J. Wyner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theoretical foundations of machine learning including why boosting and random forests work, information theory, and entropy. Directs Wharton Sports Analytics and Business Initiative. PhD from Stanford.

faculty.name: Nancy R. Zhang
faculty.title: Ge Li and Ning Zhao Professor and Vice Dean of Wharton Doctoral Programs
faculty.summary: Develops statistical models and computational algorithms for high-throughput genomic data including single-cell and spatial genomics, DNA copy number estimation, and intra-tumor heterogeneity analysis. Sloan Fellow (2011), David Cox Medal (2025). PhD from Stanford (2005).

faculty.name: Anderson Ye Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for group synchronization, spectral analysis, ranking from pairwise comparisons, clustering, stochastic block models, and network analysis. Sloan Research Fellowship in Mathematics (2025), NSF CAREER (2025).

faculty.name: Linda Zhao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Machine Learning and Data-Driven Decision Making
faculty.summary: Works on statistical machine learning including personalized RL, contextual bandits, post-selection inference, and network analysis of firm ownership. Research spans data-driven decision making, crowdsourcing, and nonparametric methods. PhD from Cornell.

faculty.name: Bingxin Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for large-scale genomic and biomedical data, studying brain-body connections using UK Biobank data, including genetic prediction, neuroimaging genetics, and heart-brain interactions. IMS Tweedie New Researcher Award (2025).

requirements.admission_model: Apply to one SMD PhD program per admissions year

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition scholarship, annual stipend, health insurance

faculty.name: Robert Strawderman
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on survival analysis, semiparametric methods for missing and censored data, statistical learning for risk and outcome prediction in medicine/epidemiology/public health, dynamic treatment regimes, causal inference, and statistical computing. ASA Fellow and IMS Fellow.

faculty.name: Sally Thurston
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian inference, modeling multiple outcomes, correction for measurement error bias, informative prior specification, latent variable models, hierarchical models, and models for multiple correlated exposures with environmental health applications. ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Abdus Wahed
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on personalized medicine, statistical methods for dynamic treatment regimes from sequential multiple assignment randomized trials (SMARTs), multivariate ordinal longitudinal data, survival analysis, causal inference, and semiparametric inference. ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Michael McDermott
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on order-restricted inference, methods to analyze dose-response, receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves and surfaces, methods for combining p-values, meta-analysis, missing data problems, and clinical trials methodology with applications in neurological disease. ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Changyong Feng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on multivariate survival analysis, empirical processes theory, longitudinal data analysis, and statistical methods in epidemiology and clinical trials.

faculty.name: Brent Johnson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on semi-parametric and nonparametric methods for missing data problems, causal inference, survival and longitudinal data, and dynamic treatment regimes with applications to HIV/AIDS, infusion trials, neurological and behavioral disorders.

faculty.name: Derick Peterson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on prognostic gene expression profiles from high-dimensional transcriptomic data, model selection, nonparametric estimation and inference, data-driven smoothing, censored survival data with applications in cancer, cardiology, and infectious respiratory diseases.

faculty.name: Xing Qiu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on high-dimensional omics data preprocessing, analysis, modeling and integration, diffusion tensor imaging, dynamic network analysis using ODEs and state-space models, and differential geometry applications to hypothesis testing based on correlation and covariance structures.

faculty.name: Tongtong Wu
faculty.title: Professor and Director of MS Programs
faculty.summary: Research focuses on high-dimensional data analysis, survival analysis, machine learning, computational statistics, computational biology and statistical genetics, and longitudinal data analysis.

faculty.name: Christopher Beck
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on inference concerning receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves and surfaces, change-point problems, and clinical trials with applications in orthopedics and neurological disease.

faculty.name: Tanzy Love
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on clustering and latent variable models, mixed membership models and model choice, normalization and preprocessing for gene expression and proteomics data, Bayesian models for QTLs and growth curves, and scalable parallel model-based clustering.

faculty.name: Matthew McCall
faculty.title: Associate Professor and PhD Program Director
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical genomics, bioinformatics, systems biology, and biomedical data science. Develops statistical methods for miRNA-seq data, cancer gene regulatory network estimation, adjusting for cellular composition on tissue-level gene expression, and preprocessing and analysis of microglia imaging data.

faculty.name: Samuel Norman-Haignere
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on component modeling of neural responses, neural encoding models, brain organization of speech and music perception, sound synthesis, deep learning, functional MRI, and human intracranial electrophysiology with emphasis on temporal integration.

faculty.name: Michael Sohn
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on high-dimensional data analysis, compositional data analysis, statistical machine learning, causal mediation analysis, and genomics and metagenomics applications.

faculty.name: Seong-Hwan Jun
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian models and computation, probabilistic machine learning, graphical models, statistical inference for combinatorial problems, single-cell transcriptomics, multi-omics data integration, cancer genomics, cancer-immune interaction, and phylogenetics.

faculty.name: Charles Wolock
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on nonparametric and semiparametric statistics, machine learning, survival analysis, statistical epidemiology, causal inference, and methods for assessing variable importance.

funding.note: Full financial support via RA/TA (tuition + monthly stipend); fellowships available

faculty.name: William Gauderman
faculty.title: Professor and Division Chief of Biostatistics and Health Data Science
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical genetics and gene-environment interaction methods, including two-step GWAS procedures and pathway polygenic risk scores. Leads the Children's Health Study on air pollution and lung development. Developed widely-used software Quanto and GxEscan. Division Chief of Biostatistics and Health Data Science.

faculty.name: Kimberly Siegmund
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chief of Education in Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Research focuses on developing mathematical models to understand cancer growth and spread, statistical analysis of DNA methylation and gene expression data for identifying disease sub-types, and design and analysis of family studies for gene characterization. Approaches questions about aging through modeling cell division processes.

faculty.name: Todd Alonzo
faculty.title: Professor of Research in Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical methods for analysis of biomarkers and medical diagnostic and screening tests, clinical trials, and design and analysis of pediatric acute myelogenous leukemia studies. Group Statistician for Children's Oncology Group and ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Daniel Stram
faculty.title: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Environmental Epidemiology, Radiation Epidemiology, Cancer Genetics
faculty.summary: Research spans environmental epidemiology, radiation exposure health effects, and cancer genetics across diverse populations. Senior biostatistician for the Multiethnic Cohort Study, chairs the EPA Radiation Advisory Committee. Published studies on air pollution and breast cancer, ultrafine particles and lung cancer, and radiation dose-mortality risk. AAAS Fellow.

faculty.name: David Conti
faculty.title: Professor and Kenneth T. Norris Jr. Chair in Cancer Prevention
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of statistical genetics and cancer epidemiology, focusing on polygenic risk scores across diverse populations, Bayesian hierarchical models for integrating multi-omic measurements, and cancer disparities. Associate Director for Data Science at USC Norris Comprehensive Cancer Center. Leads PRIMED-Cancer consortium.

faculty.name: Wendy Jean Mack
faculty.title: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences, Gerontology, and Biokinesiology and Physical Therapy. Co-Director of Biostatistics Graduate Programs in the Division of Biostatistics and Director of Biostatistics Resources at the Southern California Clinical and Translational Science Institute (SC CTSI).

faculty.name: Chun Li
faculty.title: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical genetics and genetic epidemiology, including development of the MVtest method for simultaneously modeling mean and variance of quantitative traits as functions of genotypes. Works on GWAS for blood pressure and cardiovascular traits across diverse populations including East African cohorts.

faculty.name: Paul Marjoram
faculty.title: Research Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Approximate Bayesian Computation, Population Genetics, Cancer Evolution
faculty.summary: Research spans approximate Bayesian computation (ABC) methods including the landmark MCMC-without-likelihoods paper, population genetics and coalescent theory, cancer evolution and tumor modeling, GWAS study design, and Bayesian model selection for gene regulatory networks. Developed GENETREE, MaCS, and FastCoal software.

faculty.name: Lindsay Renfro
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Research in Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Clinical Trial Design, Bayesian Adaptive Designs
faculty.summary: Research focuses on novel clinical trial designs including Bayesian adaptive, biomarker-driven, and master protocol designs (basket and umbrella trials). Associate Group Statistician for the Children's Oncology Group, specializing in pediatric cancer clinical trials, particularly Wilms tumor and Hodgkin lymphoma.

faculty.name: Joshua Millstein
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Research in Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on developing and applying statistical methods for high-dimensional multi-omic population-based studies in cancer. Specific interests include causal mediation analysis, dimensionality reduction, causal networks, false discovery rate estimation, and epistasis/gene-gene interactions. Member of USC Norris Comprehensive Cancer Center.

faculty.name: Juan Pablo Lewinger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on statistical genetics methods including gene-environment interaction (GxE) testing, hierarchical Bayes prioritization of GWAS associations, regularized hierarchical Cox regression for predictive omics, and targeted learning for GxE estimation. Extensively contributes to colorectal cancer genetics consortia.

faculty.name: Kelly Street
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on computational methods for high-dimensional genomic data, particularly single-cell RNA sequencing. Developer of the widely-used Slingshot R/Bioconductor package for cell lineage and pseudotime inference. Works on multi-omic integration, immune profiling, and chromatin structure analysis.

faculty.name: Donald Barkauskas
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Clinical Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on clinical trials methodology and oncology statistics, serving as Senior Statistician for the Children's Oncology Group. Works on the design and analysis of pediatric cancer clinical trials.

faculty.name: Sandrah Proctor Eckel
faculty.title: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental biostatistics and air pollution epidemiology. Develops and applies statistical methods for studying health effects of environmental exposures including air pollution and its constituents.

faculty.name: Eric Kawaguchi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on gene-environment interaction methods including two-step hypothesis testing for GxE in survival settings and targeted learning approaches. Collaborates on colorectal cancer genetics and environmental health studies.

faculty.name: Lingyun Ji
faculty.title: Professor of Population and Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research focuses on biostatistics for clinical trials, particularly in pediatric oncology. Faculty statistician for the Children's Oncology Group, working on design and analysis of pediatric cancer studies.

faculty.name: James G. Scott
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian statistics, machine learning, and probabilistic methods for large-scale data analysis. Serves as Department Chair of Statistics and Data Sciences at UT Austin.

faculty.name: Peter Muller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian nonparametrics, adaptive clinical trial design, Bayesian bioinformatics, and computational statistics. Joint appointments in Mathematics and the Oden Institute. Elected Fellow of IMS, ASA, and ISBA; recipient of the Zellner Medal from ISBA and past President of ISBA. Adjunct professor at MD Anderson Cancer Center.

faculty.name: Mevin Hooten
faculty.title: Professor and PhD Graduate Advisor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on spatial statistics, ecological statistics, and Bayesian hierarchical models for complex environmental and ecological data. Serves as Graduate Advisor for the PhD program and GSC Chair.

faculty.name: Jay Bartroff
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on sequential analysis, multiple testing procedures, and Bayesian decision theory. Serves as Associate Chair of the Department of Statistics and Data Sciences and Faculty Advisor for the Undergraduate Major.

faculty.name: Catherine (Kate) Calder
faculty.title: Professor and Vice Provost for Academic Administration
faculty.sub_field: Spatial Statistics, Bayesian Modeling, Network Analysis
faculty.summary: Research focuses on spatial statistics, Bayesian modeling and computation, and statistical network analysis for complex structured data. Uses geographic location data to understand how routine activity patterns and social exposures affect adolescent health. Elected Fellow of ASA and AAAS. Served as department chair from 2019-2025, during which the department tripled its tenure-track faculty.

faculty.name: Roger Peng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental health statistics, particularly the health impacts of air pollution. Demonstrated that even very low levels of air pollution are statistically linked to increased hospitalizations and deaths from cardiovascular disease. Known for work in reproducible research.

faculty.name: Mingyuan Zhou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of Bayesian statistics and machine learning, including deep generative models, reinforcement learning, and Bayesian nonparametrics. Treasurer of the Bayesian Nonparametrics Section of ISBA. Area chair for NeurIPS, ICLR, and AAAI. Joint appointment with IROM.

faculty.name: Layla Parast
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on surrogate marker evaluation, causal inference, and biostatistics. Develops methods to improve medical treatment and clinical trial outcomes. Developed the R packages cohortsurr and etsi for surrogate marker analysis.

faculty.name: Purnamrita Sarkar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on large-scale statistical machine learning, statistical models for networks, asymptotic theory, and scalable inference algorithms. Co-leads the NSF TRIPODS institute on Foundations of Data Science at UT Austin. Works on streaming PCA, network subsampling, community detection, variational inference, and the network jackknife.

faculty.name: Antonio Linero
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian nonparametrics, Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (BART) methods including the SoftBart R package, causal inference, and missing data. Recipient of an NSF CAREER Award for foundations of Bayesian nonparametric causal inference. Works on mediation analysis, survival analysis, and variable selection.

faculty.name: Yang Ni
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Causal Discovery, Bayesian Statistics, Graphical Models
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of statistics, machine learning, AI, biology, and health. Focuses on Bayesian causal discovery for gene regulatory networks, Bayesian nonparametric modeling, graphical models for nonlinear causal discovery, and reinforcement learning. Applications in multi-omics data and electronic health records. Associate Editor for JASA and Bayesian Analysis.

faculty.name: Abhra Sarkar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian nonparametric and semiparametric methods for complex data, with applications in auditory and vocal communication neuroscience (analyzing animal vocalization sequences), nutritional epidemiology (longitudinal metabolomics), and imaging neuroscience. Recipient of the Mitchell Prize from ISBA for outstanding applied Bayesian analysis.

faculty.name: Nhat Ho
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on heterogeneity of data, interpretability of models, and stability and scalability of optimization and sampling algorithms. Postdoctoral training at UC Berkeley under Michael I. Jordan and Martin J. Wainwright. PhD in Statistics from University of Michigan.

faculty.name: Amanda Glazer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on causal inference and nonparametric methods with applications in sports analytics. Previously worked with the San Francisco Giants. PhD in Statistics from UC Berkeley.

faculty.name: Yuling Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Bayesian Computation, Machine Learning, Causal Inference
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Bayesian computation, machine learning, and causal inference. Joined UT Austin SDS as part of the 2024 faculty cohort.

faculty.name: Arya Farahi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans astrostatistics (cosmology, two-point correlation function estimation), data science for social applications including transportation and housing. Director of the D3 Lab (Data, Discovery, Decision) and co-PI at the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs ($37,080/yr stipend + tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Karen Kafadar
faculty.title: Commonwealth Professor
faculty.sub_field: Robust Statistics and Exploratory Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Research focuses on robust methods and exploratory data analysis with applications to physical, chemical, engineering, and biological sciences, including genomics, forensic science, randomized cancer screening trials, spatial data, and particle physics experiments.

faculty.name: Jianhui Zhou
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.sub_field: Dimension Reduction and Functional Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Research interests include dimension reduction, robust statistics, longitudinal data analysis, functional data analysis, quantile regression, and growth curve modeling. Has advised eight PhD students with placements at Facebook, Amazon, Yahoo Research, and other industry roles.

faculty.name: Jeff Holt
faculty.title: Professor, General Faculty, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Professor of General Faculty and Director of Graduate Studies. Holds a PhD in Mathematics from University of Texas at Austin. Primarily a teaching and administrative role; no research profile or research interests listed on departmental page.

faculty.name: Jordan Rodu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include statistical visualization, theory of reasoning with data, and time series. Works in the fuzzy space between statistics and machine learning/AI.

faculty.name: Martin Slawski
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research combines statistics and computer science to address contemporary data science challenges, including high-dimensional data analysis, data integration and record linkage (with connections to data privacy), permuted/mismatched data problems, data compression and dimensionality reduction, biometric recognition, sparse signal recovery, and matrix factorization. Applied to health and engineering domains with NSF, NIH, and National Institute of Justice funding.

faculty.name: Dan Spitzner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Bayesian Statistics and Mixed Methods Research
faculty.summary: Research focuses on coordinated analysis of diverse data forms and conceptions of statistical knowledge that integrate with alternative inquiry modes including qualitative and mixed methods. Technical work in Bayesian statistics includes model choice techniques for high-dimensional analysis and clustering problems, communicating strength of evidence, functional data analysis, and classical shrinkage estimation. Interested in application of statistical methods in socially inclusive research.

faculty.name: Gretchen Martinet
faculty.title: Associate Professor, General Faculty, Director of Undergraduate Programs
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of General Faculty and Director of Undergraduate Programs. Holds a PhD in Statistics from University of Virginia. Primarily a teaching and administrative role; no research profile or research interests listed on departmental page.

faculty.name: Yong Ming Jeffrey Woo
faculty.title: Associate Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of General Faculty. Holds a PhD in Statistics from Pennsylvania State University. Primarily a teaching role; no research profile or research interests listed on departmental page.

faculty.name: Shounak Chattopadhyay
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on developing scalable, structured, and interpretable Bayesian methods, with emphasis on Bayesian nonparametrics. Works on incorporating prior information, bypassing computational bottlenecks, and obtaining provable theoretical guarantees. Member of the OHDSI community and passionate about delivering reliable clinical evidence from massive observational healthcare data-driven studies.

faculty.name: Chunlin Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research is centered on causal inference and its intersections with machine learning and public health applications. Previously an Assistant Professor at Iowa State University. PhD in Statistics from University of Minnesota, Twin Cities (2022).

faculty.name: Zachary Lubberts
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans statistics on graphs, natural language processing, and signal processing with wavelet frames for signals with strong directional content. Recent work includes vertex misalignment and changepoint localization in network time series, recovering manifold structure in LLM responses, joint cluster recovery, Procrustes problems on random matrices, and joint spectral clustering in multilayer degree-corrected stochastic blockmodels (published in JASA). Also funded by CosmicAI for merger t

faculty.name: Lingxiao Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of survey methodology, biostatistics, and epidemiology. Develops pseudo-weighting methods for nonprobability samples, model-assisted calibration weighting, and methods for integrating epidemiologic cohort data with national health surveys (NHIS, NHANES) and cancer registries. Builds nationally representative lung cancer risk models for screening and estimates pure risks for minority subgroups. Former Research Fellow at NCI/NIH.

faculty.name: Jingming Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: High-Dimensional Statistics and Random Matrix Theory
faculty.summary: Research interests include high-dimensional statistics, random matrix theory, social network analysis, statistical text mining, and applications to genomics and bioinformatics. Previously a Postdoctoral Fellow at Harvard University advised by Prof. Zheng (Tracy) Ke. PhD from HKUST (2021) under Prof. Zhigang Bao.

faculty.name: Shan Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Machine Learning and AI for Complex Data
faculty.summary: Research develops statistical, machine learning, and AI methodologies for large-scale, complex-structured data with applications to precision medicine, public health, and epidemiology. Emphasizes trustworthy and interpretable AI with rigorous statistical inference. Methodological toolkit includes non- and semi-parametric inference, distributed learning, probabilistic generative modeling, causal learning, functional data analysis, and spatial/spatiotemporal data modeling. PhD in Statistics from I

faculty.name: Taylor Brown
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Research interests include Bayesian statistics, state space models, particle filtering, and Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithms. Published work on Bayesian forecasting algorithms for time series, particle filtering (including a C++ library PF), particle swarm algorithms for posterior predictive distributions, correlated pseudo-marginal sampling, and multivariate stochastic volatility models with Particle MCMC. PhD in Statistics from University of Virginia (2018).

faculty.name: Kristen Roland
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of General Faculty. Holds a PhD in Mathematics Education with a concentration in Statistics Education from University of Georgia. MS in Statistics from University of Rhode Island. Primarily a teaching role focused on statistics education.

faculty.name: Richard Ross
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of General Faculty. Holds a PhD and MS in Statistics from University of Georgia. Primarily a teaching role; no research profile or research interests listed on departmental page.

faculty.name: Harsimran (Sam) Somal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Research interests include computational statistics, actuarial science, and applications in defense. Holds MS/PhD in Statistics from University of Iowa, BS/MA in Actuarial Science from Ball State University, and BS in Biomedical Engineering from Northwestern University.

faculty.name: Justin Weinstock
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, General Faculty
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of General Faculty. Holds a PhD and MS in Statistics and BA in Statistics and Spanish, all from University of Virginia. Primarily a teaching role; no research profile or research interests listed on departmental page.

funding.note: Fully funded (~$40,000/yr assistantship + full tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Jeffrey Blume
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Internationally recognized for contributions to statistical inference and prediction science, particularly in evaluating diagnostic and screening methods. Invented second-generation p-values that resolve mismatches in classical p-value usage. Directs the PRISM Lab, mentoring PhD students on methods bridging statistical innovation and biomedical applications including cancer screening, clinical trials, radiology, nephrology, and neuroimaging.

faculty.name: Donald Brown
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Senior Advisor to the Dean and Quantitative Foundation Distinguished Professor. Research spans data fusion, knowledge discovery, and simulation optimization, with recent publications on deep learning for histopathology classification, genomic region set embeddings, and medical image classification. Co-directs the Translational Health Institute of Virginia and serves on the board of the AI3C healthcare AI coalition.

faculty.name: YY Ahn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Distinguished Professor whose work spans network science, machine learning, and AI, with applications to computational social science, computational neuroscience and biology, and the science of science. Previously a professor at Indiana University and visiting professor at MIT. Recipient of the Microsoft Research Faculty Fellowship and LinkedIn Economic Graph Challenge.

faculty.name: Sallie Keller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Distinguished Professor and Director of the Social & Decision Analytics Division at the Biocomplexity Institute. Research expertise encompasses social and decision informatics, statistical foundations of data science, and data access and confidentiality. Previously led the U.S. Census Bureau's Research and Methodology Directorate and directed the IDA Science and Technology Policy Institute. Elected member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Aidong Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Thomas M. Linville Professor of Computer Science with joint appointments in Biomedical Engineering and Data Science. Develops machine learning approaches for interpretable and fair learning, concept-based learning, federated learning, and generative AI. Also investigates large language models for hypothesis generation in scientific discovery. Authored over 400 research publications.

faculty.name: Peter Beling
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Data Science whose work centers on resilient, mission-aligned AI at the intersection of systems theory and artificial intelligence. Studies large language models, reinforcement learning, and co-design of algorithms with the systems they operate within. Research supports national security applications including mission engineering, test and evaluation, and predictive maintenance. Affiliate of UVA's National Security Data and Policy Institute.

faculty.name: Aaron Abrams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mathematician with interests originating in geometry, topology, combinatorics, and probability. Primary current focus is two-dimensional tilings. Earlier work on configuration spaces has been highly influential, leading to applications in robotics. Received the Lester R. Ford award for mathematical exposition on a study of lotteries.

faculty.name: John Van Horn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Distinguished Professor with joint appointment in Psychology. Research centers on human neuroimaging and biomarkers in brain health and disease, treating the brain as a data science. Employs high-performance computation, multivariate statistical modeling, time series and spectral analysis, and network/graph theory. Fellow of the Organization for Human Brain Mapping. Published in Science, Nature, and Nature Neuroscience.

faculty.name: Stephen Baek
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geometric Deep Learning and Scientific ML
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Associate Professor and applied geometer who studies the space of shapes using machine learning. Research spans geometric data analysis, geometric deep learning, scientific machine learning, and data-driven design. Published work includes physics-aware deep learning for multiphase flows, ML for energetic materials design, GANs for microstructure synthesis, and CNNs on arbitrary surfaces.

faculty.name: Sheng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Associate Professor directing the Reasoning and Knowledge Discovery (RISE) Laboratory. Develops intelligent systems for open and dynamic environments, with research spanning trustworthy machine learning (robustness, fairness, causality, transferability), generative AI (LLMs, diffusion models), computer vision, and causal inference. Recent publications at ICML, CVPR, ICLR, NeurIPS, and AAAI.

faculty.name: Michael Porter
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Data scientist, statistician, and engineer directing the Event Analytics and Statistical Learning Lab. Research centers on statistical modeling of event data with three methodological pillars: event prediction and forecasting, pattern and anomaly detection, and data linkage. Applications span transplant medicine, transportation safety, criminology, environmental science, and security, emphasizing interpretability and practical decision-making impact.

faculty.name: Thomas Stewart
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor and Assistant Dean for Academic Affairs, also serving as Program Director for the Ph.D. in Data Science. Specializes in biostatistics, clinical trials, and clinical research education. Serves as lead statistician for two NIH-funded multi-site clinical trials: Pass It On (convalescent plasma for COVID-19) and ACTIV-6 (repurposed medications for COVID-19).

faculty.name: Brian Wright
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Associate Professor and Director of Undergraduate Programs. Research emphasizes data science education pedagogy, developing and studying innovative teaching methods for data science. Directs the Collaboratory for the Advancement of Data Science and Education and the Data Science Active Learning Lab. Previously built the MS in Data Science program at George Washington University.

faculty.name: Jonathan Kropko
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative Foundation Associate Professor directing the online MSDS program. Research spans civic technology, data engineering, applied statistics, and political behavior. Recent publications include work on math word problem generation with LLMs (MATHWELL) and math reasoning in language models. Leads Code for Charlottesville, a volunteer tech group working with local nonprofits and government.

faculty.name: Daniel Graham
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor whose work sits at the intersection of data science, cybersecurity, and software engineering. Research includes penetration testing, network security, machine learning for cyber defense, embedded systems and wearable prototyping, brain-computer interfaces for robot control, and smartphone-based sensing. Author of an international bestseller on ethical hacking and co-author of the second edition of Metasploit.

faculty.name: Chirag Agarwal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor leading the Aikyam lab, focused on developing trustworthy machine learning frameworks that go beyond training models for specific downstream tasks while ensuring explainability, fairness, and robustness. Research spans explainability, fairness, robustness, privacy, and transferability estimation for large-scale models. Publications at NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, with Spotlight/Oral presentations at multiple top venues.

faculty.name: Tom Hartvigsen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working to make machine learning trustworthy, robust, and socially responsible for deployment in high-stakes, dynamic settings. Research spans model editing and lifelong learning, time series explainability, hate speech detection, and early time series classification. Served as General Chair for the Machine Learning for Health Symposium 2023. Previously a Postdoctoral Associate at MIT CSAIL.

faculty.name: Teague Henry
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor with dual appointments in Data Science and Psychology. Develops new statistical techniques for modeling network and dynamical systems data, applied to personalized medicine, neuroimaging, and predictive modeling. Research interests include understanding neural correlates of neurodevelopmental disorders and designing personalized interventions for psychological disorders.

faculty.name: Jordan Bryan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor specializing in Bayesian statistics, robust estimation, and information-assisted hypothesis testing. Research spans multivariate data analysis with applications to environmental monitoring, high-energy physics, and cancer genomics. Recent publications include subscedastic weighted least squares, LLM-derived information for genomics hypothesis testing, and semiparametric canonical correlation analysis. Secretary of the junior section of ISBA.

faculty.name: Alex Gates
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Network Science and Science of Science
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor and network scientist examining how patterns of connectivity influence human behavior, decision-making, and complex system dynamics. Current focus is the Science of Science, analyzing how organizational dynamics and strategic decisions influence innovation, creativity, and success. Research spans science, art, and business, blending computational social science, network science, and sociological theory.

faculty.name: Antonios Mamalakis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor with joint appointment in Environmental Sciences. Applies statistical and Bayesian analysis, machine/deep learning, and explainable AI to environmental challenges. Research focuses on improving predictive skill for hydroclimate and extreme events, understanding climate teleconnections, advancing climate attribution and causal discovery, and investigating fidelity of XAI tools for geoscience. Associate Editor for AMS journal Artificial Intelligence for the Earth Systems.

faculty.name: Aiying Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor focusing on statistical modeling, multimodal neuroimaging, and genetics, with emphasis on graphical models and network science. Develops data-driven approaches leveraging multi-level information to understand typical and atypical brain development. Collaborates on projects involving psychiatric and neurological disorders. Previously a Postdoctoral Research Scientist at Columbia University and NYS Psychiatric Institute.

faculty.name: Javier Rasero
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor and physicist developing and applying new pipelines to efficiently integrate multimodal brain data for predicting and explaining individual differences in healthy and neuropathological populations. Built predictive models of cardiovascular disease risk factors from neuroimaging data. Strong advocate for open-source philosophy, releasing several freely available toolboxes for brain data analysis. Enthusiastic about translating methodological pipelines into clinical settings.

faculty.name: Heman Shakeri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor leading Dynamo Lab, affiliated with the UVA Center of Diabetes Technology and UVA Cancer Center. Research focuses on algorithm development at the intersection of machine learning, learning-based control, and network science, with applications to diabetes technology and cancer research. Previously analyzed multi-drug resistance networks and their evolution as a postdoc at Kansas State University.

faculty.name: Nur Yildirim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor developing human-centered and participatory approaches throughout the AI development lifecycle to translate AI technologies into products and services that benefit people and society. Research spans multimodal healthcare AI for radiology, AI in intensive care, design resources for AI ideation, human-AI guidelines, and cross-functional collaboration in AI fairness. Previously worked at Google Research and Microsoft Research. Named AI Rising Star by Michigan AI Lab in 2023.

faculty.name: Abel Rodriguez
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian statistical methods for complex problems in biology, social sciences, and engineering. Research includes Bayesian nonparametric methods, spatio-temporal modeling, network analysis, extreme value theory, and latent variable models. PhD from Duke University (2007); joined UW in 2020 after serving as professor at UC Santa Cruz.

faculty.name: Daniela M. Witten
faculty.title: Professor (Dorothy Gilford Endowed Chair in Mathematical Statistics)
faculty.summary: Develops statistical machine learning methods for high-dimensional data, with emphasis on unsupervised learning. Recent work on 'data thinning' enables valid post-selection inference by creating independent training and test sets from a single dataset. Applications span genomics, neuroscience, and microbial ecology. Co-author of the popular textbook 'Introduction to Statistical Learning' and recipient of the COPSS Presidents' Award.

faculty.name: Thomas S. Richardson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methodology for causal inference using graphical models. Co-creator of Single World Intervention Graphs (SWIGs) unifying graphical and potential outcome frameworks, and developer of ancestral graph models and nested Markov models for causal systems with unmeasured confounding. Received the 2022 Rousseeuw Prize for Statistics. Holds adjunct appointments in Economics and Electrical Engineering.

faculty.name: Jon Wakefield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian hierarchical models for spatial epidemiology and demography, with focus on small area estimation of health indicators in low- and middle-income countries. Led WHO's COVID-19 excess mortality estimation (14.8 million excess deaths globally). Extensive work on subnational child mortality estimation for UN Sustainable Development Goals. Fellow of the ASA and recipient of the Guy Medal in Bronze from RSS.

faculty.name: Tyler H. McCormick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for measuring social systems and improving population health decision-making, particularly when data are sparsely observed or measured with error. Leads the OpenVA project for verbal autopsy cause-of-death inference. Research spans social network modeling, demographic estimation from survey data, uncertainty quantification, and interpretable machine learning. Elected ASA Fellow (2023).

faculty.name: Adrian Dobra
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Bayesian Graphical Models and Health Analytics
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian methodology for high-dimensional multivariate spatiotemporal models and graphical models. Applied research focuses on human mobility using smartphone geolocation data for HIV prevention and public health. Also works on statistical disclosure limitation, COVID-19 testing strategies, and genomics network analysis. Director of the UW Master of Science in Data Science program. Elected ASA Fellow (2023).

faculty.name: Elena A. Erosheva
faculty.title: Professor (joint with School of Social Work)
faculty.sub_field: Latent Variable Models and Social Statistics
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian statistical methodology for social, behavioral, and health sciences, with expertise in mixed membership and latent variable models. Leading researcher on fairness and bias detection in grant peer review (NIH Peer Review Challenge winner). Recent work on gender homophily in scientific collaborations. Director of the Center for Statistics and the Social Sciences (CSSS). Elected ASA Fellow (2021).

faculty.name: Zaid Harchaoui
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of statistics and computing, developing robust statistical machine learning methods and computationally-efficient optimization algorithms. Studies AI foundation models (LLMs, vision models, multimodal) from a statistical perspective, including zero-shot prediction and transformer architectures. Co-founder of the NSF AI Institute on Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML). Holds adjunct appointments in CSE and Mathematics.

faculty.name: Ali Shojaie
faculty.title: Professor (joint with Biostatistics)
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Network Analysis and High-Dimensional Inference
faculty.summary: Develops statistical machine learning methods for network analysis and high-dimensional data, with applications in biology and neuroscience. Research includes estimation and inference of directed and undirected graphical models, high-dimensional time series for brain connectivity, and multi-omics data integration. Leads the Data Management and Statistics Core at the UW Alzheimer's Disease Research Center. Elected Fellow of ASA and IMS; recipient of the 2022 Leo Breiman Award. Joined UW Statistic

faculty.name: Fang Han
faculty.title: Professor (Job & Gertrude Tamaki Endowed Professor)
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical foundations for nonparametric and high-dimensional statistics, including rank- and graph-based dependence measures, statistical optimal transport (sliced Wasserstein distances), mixture models, and random matrix theory. Also works on causal inference methodology including matching estimators. Applications in single-cell genomics and wearable device data. Bernoulli Society New Researcher Award (2021). Editor for Annals of Statistics and Bernoulli.

faculty.name: Thomas R. Fleming
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus (joint with Biostatistics)
faculty.summary: Pioneering researcher in clinical trial design, conduct, and analysis. Co-author of the landmark text 'Counting Processes and Survival Analysis.' Has served on 300+ clinical trial Data Monitoring Committees and as Special Government Employee for the FDA for 30+ years. Senior author on landmark 2011 NEJM HIV prevention paper recognized as Science's 'Breakthrough of the Year.' Elected to the National Academy of Medicine.

faculty.name: Yen-Chi Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nonparametric Statistics and Geometric Data Analysis
faculty.summary: Develops nonparametric statistical methods with geometric perspectives, including topological data analysis, density ridges, and mode clustering. Research also covers causal inference with continuous treatments, nonmonotone missing data with pattern graphs, and bootstrap methods. Applications include cosmology (cosmic web reconstruction), Alzheimer's disease neuropathology, and GPS mobility analysis. NSF CAREER Award (2022) and ASA Noether Early Career Scholar Award (2022).

faculty.name: Carlos Cinelli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops causal and statistical methods for transparent and robust causal claims, with emphasis on sensitivity analysis, quasi-experimental designs, and the intersection of causality with machine learning. Creator of the widely-used sensemakr R package for sensitivity analysis. PhD from UCLA advised by Judea Pearl. NSF Award (2024-2027) for sensitivity analysis tools for quasi-experimental designs.

faculty.name: Ema Perkovic
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Dorothy Gilford Early Career Endowed Professor)
faculty.summary: Develops causal inference methodology from the perspective of probabilistic graphical models, focusing on covariate adjustment, causal identification in partially known graphs, and efficient estimation of causal effects. Work addresses settings with latent variables and hidden confounders, and leverages background knowledge to narrow causal effect estimates. NSF grant recipient. Program Co-Chair for UAI 2026.

faculty.name: Xinwei Shen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops distributional learning methods to estimate full conditional distributions beyond point predictions. Creator of 'engression', a generative modeling approach blending energy-based and regression frameworks for conditional distribution learning with arbitrary data types. Applications include climate downscaling, distributional causal effect estimation, and extrapolation. PhD from HKUST. Recipient of UW Royalty Research Fund Award for universal generative models.

faculty.name: Armeen Taeb
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Model Selection and Causal Structure Learning
faculty.summary: Develops methodology at the intersection of optimization and statistics. Introduced a framework for false discovery control over partially ordered sets (posets), enabling rigorous model selection beyond standard hypothesis testing (e.g., rankings, clusters, causal graphs). Also works on causal structure learning with unknown interventions and latent variables, and distributionally robust prediction using causal reasoning. PhD from Caltech; NSF Award recipient.

faculty.name: Marco Carone
faculty.title: Professor and Interim Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on causal inference methodology with applications to public health and medicine. Norman Breslow Endowed Faculty Fellow and Adjunct Professor of Statistics. Awarded the 2025 Myrto Lefkopoulou Distinguished Lectureship.

faculty.name: KC Gary Chan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Health Services Research and Causal Inference
faculty.summary: Joint appointment in Biostatistics and Health Services. Research interests span causal inference, clinical trials, semi-parametric models, and health services research with applications to cardiovascular health in Asian American, Native Hawaiian, and Pacific Islander populations.

faculty.name: Thomas Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on clinical trial methodology, survival analysis, and sequential analysis with applications in cancer and HIV/AIDS. Joint appointment in Statistics and Biostatistics. Widely published on clinical trial design and integrity including COVID-19 vaccine trial design and ethical considerations.

faculty.name: Patrick Heagerty
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on method development for longitudinal studies with emphasis on prognostic model evaluation and structural longitudinal models, plus rigorous approaches for cluster-randomized trials in healthcare settings. Co-author of Analysis of Longitudinal Data and Biostatistics: A Methodology for the Health Sciences. Directs the Center for Biomedical Statistics and the Biostatistics and Research Design Core for the NIH Health Care Systems Research Collaboratory. Elected to the National Aca

faculty.name: Lurdes Inoue
faculty.title: Professor and Gilbert S. Omenn Endowed Chair in Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Research interests include Bayesian biostatistics, clinical trials, cancer research, and decision theory. Former chair of the department. Gilbert S. Omenn Endowed Chair in Biostatistics.

faculty.name: Kathleen Kerr
faculty.title: Professor and MS Capstone Program Director
faculty.summary: Research interests include biomarkers, clinical trials, statistical genetics and genomics, experimental design, with applications in nephrology, pathology, and cardiovascular research. Serves as MS Capstone Program Director and Director of the Summer Institute in Statistics for Clinical and Epidemiological Research.

faculty.name: Brian Leroux
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Joint appointment in Biostatistics and Oral Health Sciences. Research focuses on correlated data methods, clinical trials, and epidemiological methods with applications in dentistry and substance use research.

faculty.name: Susanne May
faculty.title: Professor and Director, UW Clinical Trials Center
faculty.summary: Research focuses on clinical trial methodology, longitudinal data analysis, and survival analysis with applications in HIV/AIDS research. Serves as Director of the UW Clinical Trials Center. Has served on FDA advisory panels including for COVID-19 treatments.

faculty.name: Ken Rice
faculty.title: Professor and Graduate Program Director
faculty.sub_field: Genetic Epidemiology and Foundations of Inference
faculty.summary: Research focuses on developing and applying statistical methods for complex disease epidemiology, with emphasis on cardiovascular disease and genetic epidemiology. Also researches statistical methods for meta-analysis and the foundations of inference, notably developing connections between Bayesian and non-Bayesian methods. Co-directs the UW Genetic Analysis Center and leads the coordinating center of the NIH PRIMED consortium.

faculty.name: Andrea Rotnitzky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on developing analytical tools for estimating intervention effects from non-experimental or imperfect experimental data, spanning causal inference and missing and censored data analysis. Focuses on semiparametric efficient methods that leverage available data without unnecessary assumptions. Current work includes flexible machine learning methods for causal inference, efficient estimation in causal graphical models, and integrating data from multiple sources.

faculty.name: Lianne Sheppard
faculty.title: Professor and Rohm & Haas Endowed Professor in Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Joint appointment in Environmental and Occupational Health Sciences and Biostatistics. Research focuses on environmental and spatial statistics with applications in air pollution epidemiology, including work on particulate matter air quality standards and pesticide exposure assessment.

faculty.name: Ali Shojaie
faculty.title: Professor and Interim Chair
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Machine Learning and Network Analysis
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of statistical machine learning, statistical network analysis and applications in biology and social sciences. Elected ASA and IMS Fellow. Won the 2022 Leo Breiman Award. Serves as founding director of SISBID and Lead of the DMS Core of the UW Alzheimer's Disease Research Center.

faculty.name: Adam Szpiro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans environmental and spatial statistics, measurement error methods, Bayesian biostatistics, and correlated data analysis. Work involves air pollution epidemiology with studies on asthma, lung disease, cardiovascular outcomes, blood pressure, dementia, and neurodevelopment.

faculty.name: Nancy Temkin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Clinical Trials in Neurosurgery and Traumatic Brain Injury
faculty.summary: Research focuses on outcome assessments for a variety of treatments for neurosurgical diseases, particularly traumatic brain injury (TBI). Served as PI on the seminal studies of the value of prophylactic anticonvulsants in TBI management. Has multiple awards from the NIH and DOD.

faculty.name: Jon Wakefield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans the analysis of complex survey data, small area estimation, spatial epidemiology and space-time models for infectious disease data. Additional areas include software development, estimating excess mortality, child mortality burden at national and subnational levels, and ecological inference. Joint appointment in Statistics. Serves on the UN Child Mortality Advisory Group. COVID-19 excess mortality research received extensive global media coverage and was published in Nature.

faculty.name: Daniela Witten
faculty.title: Professor and Dorothy Gilford Endowed Chair of Mathematical Statistics
faculty.summary: Research interests include high-dimensional data, statistical learning, genomics, classification techniques, computational biology, multivariate methods, and neuroscience. Recipient of the COPSS Presidents' Award (2022), Leo Breiman Award (2021), and Mortimer Spiegelman Award (2019). Simons Investigator and IMS Fellow.

faculty.name: Peter Gilbert
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.sub_field: Vaccine Clinical Trials and Immune Correlates
faculty.summary: Extensive experience designing and analyzing clinical trials for preventive vaccines (HIV, malaria, dengue, COVID-19). Co-PI and PI of the statistical data management center for the HIV Vaccine Trials Network (HVTN). Led analyses of six HIV vaccine efficacy trials and designed the first efficacy trial of a monoclonal antibody for HIV prevention. Elected ASA Fellow. Recipient of the 2025 Marvin Zelen Leadership Award in Statistical Science.

faculty.name: Michael LeBlanc
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on clinical trial design and survival analysis with applications in oncology. Affiliated with Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center. Work includes long-term clinical trial data analysis for cancer treatments including the SWOG S1826 nivolumab-AVD trial in Hodgkin lymphoma. Elected ASA Fellow.

faculty.name: Robyn McClelland
faculty.title: Research Professor and Co-Director, Collaborative Health Studies Coordinating Center
faculty.summary: Research interests include biomarkers, clinical trials, and epidemiological methods with focus on cardiovascular disease epidemiology. Co-Director of the Collaborative Health Studies Coordinating Center. Involved in the Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis (MESA).

faculty.name: Barbra Richardson
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.sub_field: Infectious Disease Biostatistics and Missing Data Methods
faculty.summary: Over thirty years as lead statistician on numerous clinical trials and observational studies of HIV and other infectious diseases. Methods research concentrates on developing methods for analysis of infectious disease data and methods for analysis of data with missing values. Developed methodology to estimate breastmilk infectivity among HIV-1 infected mothers.

faculty.name: Amy Willis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on microbiome statistics, statistical phylogenetics, and biodiversity. Leads the StatDivLab. Recent work includes an NSF grant to develop novel methods to estimate bacterial evolutionary histories and research on the gut microbiome of people with high farm animal exposure.

faculty.name: Eardi Lila
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Functional Data Analysis and Neuroimaging Statistics
faculty.summary: Research focuses on developing statistical methodology for analyzing medical imaging data with a special interest in functional and structural neuroimaging.

faculty.name: Kevin Lin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on studying cellular mechanisms from single-cell data. Develops novel methods using ideas from matrix factorization, network modeling, and changepoint detection with the goal of deriving new insights for advancing single-cell biology.

faculty.name: Guanghao Qi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include high-dimensional data, statistical learning, and statistical genetics and genomics. Recent work involves identifying genes associated with autoimmune diseases and chasing genetic variants. PhD from Johns Hopkins University.

faculty.name: Ting Ye
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include causal inference and clinical trials. Genentech Endowed Professor (2022-23). PhD in Statistics from University of Wisconsin-Madison.

faculty.name: Bret Larget
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies Bayesian computational approaches to study molecular evolution and phylogenetics. Also heavily involved in data science education and the undergraduate data science major.

faculty.name: Cecile Ane
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for molecular and trait evolution using graphical models and stochastic processes. Creates computational tools for Bayesian inference and model selection enabling biological discovery across ecology, food science, and veterinary science.

faculty.name: Rick Chappell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in the design and analysis of clinical trials, developing and applying statistical approaches to improve how clinical trials are structured and evaluated.

faculty.name: Peter Chien
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans verification, validation and uncertainty quantification, decision-making under uncertainty, and statistical machine learning with applications in physical sciences, engineering, and industry. Work adopted by Fortune 500 companies.

faculty.name: Matthias Katzfuss
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational spatial and spatio-temporal statistics, Gaussian processes, uncertainty quantification, and data assimilation for environmental and satellite remote-sensing data. Funded by NSF, NASA, and NOAA.

faculty.name: Sunduz Keles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for genomic data analysis, focusing on high-throughput biological data, regulatory genomics, and survival-type outcomes in bioinformatics.

faculty.name: Wei-Yin Loh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Primary focus on classification and regression trees, including algorithm development, theoretical properties, and practical applications across diverse data analysis domains.

faculty.name: Michael Newton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on data science for basic and translational biomedicine, with contributions spanning cancer biology, immunology, genomics, high-dimensional biostatistical inference, and computational statistics theory.

faculty.name: Debdeep Pati
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans Bayesian theory and methods, machine learning, and high-dimensional applications including Bayesian graphical models, efficient computation, network analysis, and fair algorithms.

faculty.name: Garvesh Raskutti
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of optimization theory, information theory, and theoretical statistics, addressing computational and statistical challenges of large-scale inference problems.

faculty.name: Karl Rohe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops tools and theory to analyze large social graphs, focusing on sampling and factoring data methods. Works on principal component analysis, factor rotations, and semi-parametric factor models for network and financial time series data.

faculty.name: Jun Shao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on theoretical statistics, particularly asymptotic theory, resampling methods such as bootstrap, model selection, and inference for linear and nonlinear models with applications in survey sampling.

faculty.name: Yazhen Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans financial statistics and data science, quantum computing and tomography, high-dimensional inference, machine learning, and nonparametric methods including wavelets and change-point analysis.

faculty.name: Chunming Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methodologies for large-scale data challenges in neuroscience, genomics, and finance. Research spans neuroinformatics, bioinformatics, multiple testing, and non-parametric estimation.

faculty.name: Jun Zhu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methodology for spatially referenced data and spatio-temporal data, applied to agricultural, environmental, ecological, and health science problems including disease mapping and medical imaging.

faculty.name: Jessi Cisewski-Kehe
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Creates statistical methods for complex data where traditional models fall short, with primary emphasis on astrostatistics including detecting exoplanets via radial velocity. Also works on topological data analysis and persistent homology.

faculty.name: Nicolas Garcia Trillos
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of applied analysis, optimal transport, and machine learning, developing mathematical tools for large sample asymptotics of graph-based learning methods and their continuum PDE limits.

faculty.name: Hyunseung Kang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theory and methods for causal inference in large observational studies using instrumental variables, matching, and machine learning. Applications span genetics, epidemiology, health policy, and economics.

faculty.name: Vivak Patel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Connects numerical optimization with statistical estimation, designing statistically sound yet computationally practical estimators and optimization methods for problems involving uncertainty.

faculty.name: Miaoyan Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on statistical machine learning for matrix and tensor data analysis, along with high-dimensional statistics, nonparametric learning, and computational-statistical tradeoffs. Applies methods to genetics. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Jiwei Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans statistics, biostatistics, and medical informatics with joint appointments across departments.
