faculty.name: Sameer Deshpande
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on Bayesian hierarchical modeling, treed regression, model selection, and causal inference with real-world applications in public health and sports analytics.

faculty.name: Chris Geoga
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Models dependence structure in complex spatial and spatio-temporal data at scale, drawing on applied mathematics techniques including numerical analysis and fast linear algebra algorithms.

faculty.name: Yongyi Guo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops cutting-edge statistical learning and decision-making methods with applications in digital health interventions and dynamic real-world settings.

faculty.name: Benjamin Lengerich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops context-adaptive models to understand complex diseases and advance precision medicine, connecting data-driven discoveries to practical medical treatments.

faculty.name: Alejandra Quintos
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on applied probability and stochastic processes, developing statistical methods to model dependence and risk in financial contexts including credit risk, systemic risk, and microfinance.

faculty.name: Kris Sankaran
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical tools for exploring latent variation in microbiological and Earth systems data, working with multi-source datasets spanning multiple scales.

faculty.name: Michael A. Newton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Focuses on statistical inference in biology, developing stochastic models, nonparametric and empirical Bayesian methods, and high-dimensional inference for molecular, genetic, and genomic data analysis.

faculty.name: Karl Broman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on statistical problems in genetics and genomics, particularly methods for detecting and identifying genes contributing to variation in complex traits in experimental organisms.

faculty.name: Richard J. Chappell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in the design and analysis of clinical trials, developing statistical approaches to improve trial methodology and evaluation.

faculty.name: Mark Craven
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops machine learning methods for biological networks, inferring models of interactions among genes, proteins, metabolites, and phenotypes. Works on genotype-phenotype modeling, ontology mapping, and disease trajectory modeling.

faculty.name: Colin Dewey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational analysis of biological sequence data, including transcriptome analysis via RNA-seq, evolutionary analysis of whole genomes, and metadata standardization for machine learning on sequence databases.

faculty.name: Ron Gangnon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on spatial and spatio-temporal epidemiology, high-dimensional variable selection for cluster detection, small area estimation, and multi-state models. Collaborates across cancer, infectious disease, and environmental epidemiology.

faculty.name: Anthony Gitter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational approaches to reconstruct signaling pathways and gene regulatory networks from high-throughput data. Applies machine learning to prioritize biological experiments for drug discovery and protein engineering.

faculty.name: Sunduz Keles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for genomic data analysis, with a focus on high-throughput biological data and regulatory genomics.

faculty.name: Christina Kendziorski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on statistical methods and software for computational biology and genomics, developing quantitative approaches to analyze genomic data across diverse biological contexts.

faculty.name: Sushmita Roy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods based on statistical machine learning for understanding gene regulatory networks from genomic data, spanning cell fate specification, host-microbe interactions, and disease processes.

faculty.name: Vikas Singh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for interpreting visual data, combining statistical and algorithmic approaches to advance medical imaging, computer vision, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Moo Chung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational neuroimaging using non-invasive brain imaging techniques (MRI, fMRI, DTI) to study spatio-temporal brain dynamics. Applies geometric and topological data analysis to neuroimaging.

faculty.name: Thomas D. Cook
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in the Department of Biostatistics and Medical Informatics focusing on clinical trials methodology and biostatistical applications.

faculty.name: Guanhua Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methods for clinical and biomedical research, analyzing high-dimensional data from genomics and electronic health records to advance precision medicine.

faculty.name: Yin Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies computer vision techniques to healthcare applications and advances mobile health and wearable computing technologies for medical contexts.

faculty.name: Qiongshi Lu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for human genetics research, with emphasis on genome-wide association studies, gene-environment interaction, and genetic risk prediction.

faculty.name: Lu Mao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on causal inference, missing or censored data, semiparametric theory, and clinical trials, with applications in diagnostic radiology and cardiovascular disease.

faculty.name: Irene Ong
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies data mining, AI, machine learning, and probabilistic methods to biological and medical data, integrating diverse molecular and clinical data types to advance precision medicine.

faculty.name: Zhengzheng Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for high-dimensional omics data, including genetic association studies and microbiome analysis.

faculty.name: Daifeng Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops machine learning and bioinformatics tools to analyze multimodal data for genotype-phenotype prediction and understanding gene regulation in the human brain, with focus on brain diseases.

faculty.name: Jiwei Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans biostatistics and medical informatics, with joint appointments in both Statistics and Biostatistics & Medical Informatics.

faculty.name: Mary Baumann
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on correlated data and the design and analysis of clinical trials, with specific applications to Alzheimer's disease research and complex data structures.

faculty.name: Daniel Pimentel-Alarcon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Creates theory and algorithms for handling messy data with outliers, missing values, noise, and small sample sizes. Explores theoretical boundaries of learning from imperfect data, with applications from biomedicine to astronomy.

faculty.name: Yu Shyr
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Focuses on big data analytics in biomedical research, biostatistical and bioinformatic methodologies for cancer research, high-dimensional data preprocessing, RNA-sequencing, predictive modeling, biomarkers, personalized medicine, and adaptive clinical trial design.

faculty.name: Frank E. Harrell Jr.
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Founding Chair. Research spans Bayesian statistics, predictive modeling and validation, ordinal response models, nonparametrics and spline functions, survival analysis, bootstrap methods, and clinical trials methodology. Applications in cardiovascular disease and pharmaceutical safety.

faculty.name: Bryan E. Shepherd
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vice Chair of Faculty Affairs. Research in causal inference, measurement error correction, sensitivity analyses, and missing data methods, with applications in global health, HIV/AIDS, and tuberculosis. Works on synthetic data methods for multinational HIV cohorts.

faculty.name: Jonathan S. Schildcrout
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vice Chair for Research. Research spans longitudinal data analysis, electronic health records, causal inference, predictive modeling, and clinical trials methodology, with applications in cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and health services research.

faculty.name: Tatsuki Koyama
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on group sequential methods, two-stage adaptive procedures for phase II/III trials, noninferiority and equivalence trials, predictive modeling, and statistical graphics. Applications in cancer, infectious disease, and gastroenterology.

faculty.name: Qingxia Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vice Chair of Education. Research in Bayesian statistics, bioinformatics, electronic health records, longitudinal analysis, and survival analysis with applications in cancer, cardiovascular disease, ophthalmology, and infectious disease.

faculty.name: Benjamin French
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on cardiovascular disease epidemiology, randomized clinical trials, longitudinal data analysis, and risk prediction, with applications in pediatrics and real-world data.

faculty.name: Leena Choi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vice Chair of Outreach. Research in Bayesian statistics, bioinformatics, clinical trials methodology, electronic health records, likelihood methods, and longitudinal analysis with applications in pharmacology.

faculty.name: Robert Alan Greevy Jr.
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Biostatistics, Center for Health Services Research. Focuses on causal inference, observational study design, and health services research with applications in cardiovascular disease, diabetes, nephrology, and pharmacology.

faculty.name: Qi Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Director of Bioinformatics and Technical Director of VANGARD. Research in cancer bioinformatics, omics data analysis, and survival analysis, with applications in cancer, digestive disease, and infectious disease.

faculty.name: Rameela Raman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans clinical trials methodology, health policy, epidemiology, data linkage, and health services research, with applications in critical care, child welfare, and infectious disease.

faculty.name: Andrew Spieker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Graduate Studies. Focuses on causal inference, causal mediation analysis, randomized trials including mobile health interventions, longitudinal analysis, and semiparametric modeling with applications in cancer, neurology, and health policy.

faculty.name: Simon Vandekar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Graduate Recruitment. Develops statistical methods for brain imaging data, focusing on improving reproducibility in neuroimaging studies, high-dimensional analysis, and semiparametric modeling for mental health and neurodegenerative diseases.

faculty.name: Ran Tao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods for genetics and genomics using electronic health records, focusing on likelihood methods, measurement error, missing data, and case-control sampling designs in respiratory and pulmonary disease.

faculty.name: Matt Shotwell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Design & Analysis Unit at VBDCC. Research in Bayesian statistics, clinical trials, machine learning, and computational biology with applications in anesthesiology, nephrology, and pharmacology.

faculty.name: James Christopher Slaughter
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on Bayesian statistics, longitudinal data analysis, epidemiology, and ordinal response models with applications in cardiovascular disease, digestive disease, and pediatrics.

faculty.name: Dandan Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Executive Director of VBDCC. Research in event history analysis, risk prediction, diagnostic accuracy measures, and clinical trials with applications in cognitive aging, Alzheimer's disease, cardiovascular disease, and cancer.

faculty.name: Hakmook Kang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Data Science Core at Vanderbilt Kennedy Center. Focuses on high-dimensional data analysis, brain imaging statistics, and whole brain connectivity estimation using Gaussian processes.

faculty.name: Mario Davidson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Vice Chair of Outreach. Focuses on ethics in statistics and data science, medical education assessment, and health services and outcomes research.

faculty.name: Quanhu Sheng
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Director of Advanced Computing at CQS. Research in bioinformatics, cancer genomics, omics data analysis, and biobank data integration.

faculty.name: Gustavo Amorim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on global health, infectious disease, observational studies using electronic health records, measurement error, and missing data methods.

faculty.name: Amir Asiaee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on bioinformatics, cancer research, causal inference, computational biology, and machine learning applied to genetics and genomics.

faculty.name: Amber J. Hackstadt
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian methods for health services research and cardiovascular outcomes, leveraging information from clinical trials to study major adverse cardiovascular events.

faculty.name: Siyuan Ma
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in high-dimensional data analysis, biomedical image synthesis, microbiome research, and the intersection of statistics with large language models.

faculty.name: Qingyan Xiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in causal inference, clinical trials methodology, genetics and genomics, machine learning, and survival analysis with applications in neurology, pain research, and infectious disease.

faculty.name: John E. McCarthy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Spencer T. Olin Professor of Mathematics. Also holds a courtesy appointment as Professor of Statistics and Data Science. Research focuses on operator theory, complex analysis, and functional analysis.

faculty.name: Renato Feres
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry, dynamical systems, and mathematics in chemistry. Has regularly taught courses on stochastic processes and Lebesgue integration, bridging probability theory and mathematical analysis.

faculty.name: Ari Stern
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometric numerical analysis: using geometry to develop novel numerical methods for differential equations that preserve structure and provide global accuracy, relevant to computational statistics and data-driven modeling.

faculty.name: Donsub Rim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in numerical analysis and model reduction, developing computational methods for approximating complex mathematical models with simpler, more efficient representations relevant to high-dimensional data analysis.

faculty.name: Xuming He
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on robust statistical methods, quantile regression, Bayesian inference, and post-selection inference. Advocates for interdisciplinary data science and the union between AI and statistics. Fellow of ASA and AAAS, President of International Statistical Institute (2023-2025).

faculty.name: José E. Figueroa-López
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of statistics, financial mathematics, and probability, focusing on inference from high-frequency financial data, limit order books, jump-diffusion models, option pricing asymptotics, and portfolio optimization. Funded by NSF.

faculty.name: Likai Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and theory for complex high-dimensional data with temporal dependence. Works on statistical learning for time series, high-dimensional testing, changepoint detection, structured models, and robust statistics.

faculty.name: Jimin Ding
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for complex data structures where time-dependent processes interact, specializing in joint modeling of longitudinal measurements and survival outcomes. Uses nonparametric smoothing, functional data analysis, and dynamical systems. Funded by NIH.

faculty.name: Joe Guinness
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on modeling and computational issues in spatial-temporal data analysis, particularly for Earth science applications. Lead developer of the GpGp R package for Gaussian process models, with contributions to spectral methods and Vecchia approximations.

faculty.name: Ran Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theory, methodology, and applications of reinforcement learning and data-driven decision-making, with focus on revenue management and healthcare. Combines statistical machine learning with optimization.

faculty.name: Chao Cheng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Enhances robustness and efficiency of associational and causal effect estimation through semiparametric inference. Focuses on causal mediation analysis, quantile causal inference, and measurement error correction for health science applications.

faculty.name: Apratim Dey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on signal recovery via compressed sensing, digital twins, foundational issues in deep learning theory, and methods for training generative models.

faculty.name: Joe Feldman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates external information into statistical models for more accurate inference when data are incomplete or unrepresentative. Works on Bayesian methods, data privacy, missing data, and causal inference. Recognized by ASA sections on Bayesian, Government, and Health Policy statistics.

faculty.name: Hong Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Builds theoretical foundations for algorithms that extract information from high-dimensional data. Studies properties of high-dimensional random systems such as phase transitions and universality, and their implications for algorithm design.

programs.program_name: PhD in Biomedical Informatics & Data Science

requirements.admission_model: Apply to DBBS division; affiliate with program

funding.note: Fully funded; all admits receive equal support

faculty.name: Philip R. O. Payne
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of I2DB. Research focuses on AI-based discovery of bio-molecular and clinical phenotypes, EHR-based clinical decision support, human factors in health IT, and data sharing platforms for learning health systems.

faculty.name: Po-Yin Yen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Deputy Director for Education at I2DB. Research in EHR data analytics, EHR documentation analysis, visual analytics, and AI applications in healthcare.

faculty.name: Joanna Abraham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Applied Health Informatics. Research focuses on improving collaborative practices in healthcare using informatics principles, including clinical handoffs, perioperative mental health, and telemedicine.

faculty.name: Thomas Kannampallil
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Chief Data Scientist at I2DB. Research in biomedical informatics, pragmatic clinical trials, human-AI interactions, patient safety, and health AI applications.

faculty.name: Albert M. Lai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chief Research Information Officer and Deputy Director of I2DB. Research in biomedical informatics, telemedicine, natural language processing and large language models for healthcare applications.

faculty.name: Adam Wilcox
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in clinical analytics, quality improvement, AI applications in healthcare, and research methodology for clinical informatics.

faculty.name: Randi Foraker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Population Health Informatics. Research in population-based study design, EHR data integration with socioeconomic indicators, synthetic data, and clinical decision support for risk scoring in primary care and oncology.

faculty.name: Linying Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Director of CausAI Lab. Research in causal machine learning for healthcare, focusing on explainability, generalizability, and fairness of models trained on EHRs for real-world evidence generation and clinical risk prediction.

faculty.name: Lei Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Deputy Director for Faculty Affairs and Research at I2DB. Research in survival analysis, longitudinal data analysis, personalized medicine, machine learning, and high-dimensional omics data. Fellow of ASA.

faculty.name: Mackenzie Hofford
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Chief Research Information Officer. Research in biomedical informatics, predictive modeling for chronic diseases, health AI, and clinical informatics.

faculty.name: Laura K. Wiley
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational phenotyping, learning health systems, and precision medicine, using informatics approaches to improve clinical care.

faculty.name: Charles Goss
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Biostatistics and Data Science (CBDS). Research in biostatistics, clinical trials methodology, causal inference, and implementation science.

faculty.name: Inez Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in electronic health records analysis, predictive modeling, natural language processing, and large language models for healthcare applications.

faculty.name: Daphne Lew
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Clinical Trials, Social Determinants of Health
faculty.summary: Research in clinical trials methodology, longitudinal data analysis, social determinants of health, and EHR log data analysis.

faculty.name: Fuhai Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in translational bioinformatics, applying computational methods to pediatric diseases and clinical applications.

faculty.name: Ahmed Said
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in clinical decision support, predictive modeling, and translational bioinformatics for data-driven critical care and ECMO.

faculty.name: Sabine Dietmann
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in translational bioinformatics applied to developmental biology and medical applications.

faculty.name: Richard Head
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Translational Bioinformatics. Research in translational bioinformatics, applying computational methods to genetic and genomic data for clinical translation.

funding.note: Fully funded 5+ yrs (~$52k stipend + full tuition)

faculty.name: P. M. Aronow
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for causal inference using experimental and observational data, focusing on design-based approaches, interference in networks, variance estimation, and regression discontinuity designs, with applications in political science, biostatistics, and public health.

faculty.name: Andrew Barron
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of information theory, probability, and statistics, with foundational contributions to universal approximation bounds for neural networks (4,200+ citations), the minimum description length principle, capacity-achieving sparse superposition codes, and high-dimensional function estimation. Recipient of the 2024 Shannon Award.

faculty.name: Joseph Chang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans stochastic processes, Bayesian inference, Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, evolutionary tree reconstruction, and statistical genetics. Develops Bayesian and MCMC-based approaches for problems in probability and statistics, including change-point problems and applications in autism research.

faculty.name: Yuejie Chi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theoretical and algorithmic foundations of data science, generative AI, reinforcement learning, and signal processing. Current emphasis on improving the performance, efficiency and reliability of generative AI and decision making in data-intensive but resource-limited settings, including chain-of-thought reasoning, RLHF, and diffusion models.

faculty.name: Mark Gerstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads a biomedical data science lab bridging vast data generation in biomedical sciences with analytic approaches from statistics and computer science. Works on genome annotation (ENCODE), disease genomics (cancer, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder), macromolecular dynamics, interpretable machine learning, and genomic data privacy including homomorphic encryption.

faculty.name: Anna Gilbert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of analysis, probability, discrete mathematics, and algorithms, focusing on randomized algorithms for signal and image processing, sparse approximation, and compressed sensing. Known for foundational work on Orthogonal Matching Pursuit for signal recovery from random measurements (8,600+ citations).

faculty.name: John Lafferty
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pursues broad machine learning research from statistical theory to applications, best known for co-inventing Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). Current work focuses on understanding how intelligence and cognition emerge in the brain and in computational systems, spanning NeuroAI, reinforcement learning, abstract reasoning, and neural computation. Director of Yale's Center for Neurocomputation and Machine Intelligence.

faculty.name: Zongming Ma
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Multimodal Data Integration and Spatial Statistics
faculty.summary: Builds statistical foundations and scalable algorithms for joint analysis of weakly linked multimodal data, bridging spatial transcriptomics, single-cell multi-omics, and digital pathology to turn fragmented, heterogeneous measurements into calibrated inference and actionable biomedical discovery.

faculty.name: Dan Spielman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on spectral and algebraic graph theory, solving Laplacian linear equations, graph sparsification, smoothed analysis of algorithms, and the Kadison-Singer problem. Also develops methods for balancing covariates in randomized experiments via Gram-Schmidt walk design. Director of Yale's Institute for Foundations of Data Science. Recipient of the Godel Prize (2008, 2015) and Nevanlinna Prize.

faculty.name: Yihong Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of high-dimensional statistics, information theory, and optimization, focusing on information-theoretic and computational limits of statistical problems, planted structures in networks and matrices (community detection, graph matching), empirical Bayes, and functional estimation. Co-authoring the forthcoming book 'Information Theory: From Coding to Learning.' IMS Fellow and Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Huibin (Harry) Zhou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans decision theory, high-dimensional statistical inference, covariance and precision matrix estimation, Gaussian mixture models, and spectral clustering. Contributed optimality results for sparse covariance estimation, high-dimensional Gaussian mixtures, and community detection. Former department chair (2012-2021). NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Xiang Zhou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Genetics and Genomic Data Science
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and machine learning methods including deep learning and AI for large-scale genetic and genomic data analysis. Works on GWAS, molecular QTL mapping, single-cell and spatial omics to understand how genomic variation influences biological functions and contributes to phenotypic variation in human diseases and complex traits.

faculty.name: Zhou Fan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Random Matrix Theory and High-Dimensional Statistics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of mathematical statistics, probability theory, and computational algorithms. Research focuses on random matrix theory, high-dimensional statistics, methods inspired by statistical physics (AMP, replica method), and applications in statistical genetics and cryo-electron microscopy. NSF CAREER Award and Arthur Greer Memorial Prize recipient.

faculty.name: Sekhar Tatikonda
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans information theory, communications, networking, and statistical inference. Contributed to feedback channel coding theory using directed information and Markov decision processes, sparse regression codes, network tomography via phylogenetic inference methods, and convergence analysis of belief propagation on graphs with cycles. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Johan Ugander
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Computational Social Science and Network Science
faculty.summary: Uses statistical and computational methods to study social networks, human behavior, and their interplay. Research spans causal inference under network interference, discrete choice theory and preference learning, information diffusion mechanisms, voting systems, and algorithmic social systems including peer review and ranking models. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Sinho Chewi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Sampling, Optimal Transport, and Diffusion Models
faculty.summary: Broadly interested in the mathematics of machine learning and statistics, focusing on the complexity of log-concave sampling (writing a book on this topic), optimal transport with applications to variational inference, and theory for score-based diffusion generative models. Works span Langevin algorithms, mean-field variational inference, and information-theoretic lower bounds.

faculty.name: Roy Lederman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Mathematics of Data Science and Cryo-EM
faculty.summary: Works on mathematics of data science combining inverse problems and unsupervised learning, with key applications in structural biology (cryo-EM heterogeneity analysis and hyper-molecules), Bayesian inference (Riemannian Hamiltonian Monte Carlo), manifold learning (Alternating Diffusion for multi-sensor data), and computational biology (randomized DNA/RNA search algorithms).

faculty.name: Shuangping Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include probability theory, high-dimensional statistics, theoretical machine learning, and the theory of algorithms, with work spanning spectral clustering, Gaussian mixture models, binary perceptron models, and random graph analysis.

faculty.name: Lu Lu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Scientific Machine Learning and AI for Science
faculty.summary: Leads a group focused on scientific machine learning (SciML) and AI for science (AI4Science), developing theory, algorithms, and software for modeling and simulating physical and biological systems. Research spans physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), neural operators for PDEs, diffusion models for inverse problems, and scientific foundation models.

faculty.name: Theo McKenzie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Spectral Geometry, Random Graphs, and Random Matrices
faculty.summary: Researches spectral geometry of discrete systems, studying random matrices and random graphs using analytic and probabilistic methods. Key results include the Ramanujan property and edge universality of random regular graphs, optimal eigenvalue rigidity, quantum ergodicity for periodic graphs, and constructions of high-girth near-Ramanujan expanders.

faculty.name: Theodor Misiakiewicz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical foundations for modern machine learning paradigms, with particular focus on deep learning theory. Research spans kernel and random feature methods in high dimensions, non-convex optimization and landscape analysis, computational learning theory, and random matrix theory. Authored lecture notes on mathematics of deep learning and computational complexity of deep learning.

faculty.name: Omar Montasser
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Theory of Machine Learning and Robustness
faculty.summary: Explores theory and foundations of machine learning with focus on robustness to adversarial examples and distribution shifts. Research spans adversarial robustness, distribution learning and score matching, LLM hallucination detection via chain-of-thought supervision, multi-distribution learning, and transformation-invariant learning.

faculty.name: Zhuoran Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of machine learning, statistics, game theory, and optimization. Primary focus areas are foundations of reinforcement learning in multi-agent systems where agents interact strategically, and understanding emergent behaviors of large language models during pre-training and post-training and their relationship to model architecture.

faculty.name: Ilias Zadik
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of high-dimensional statistics, theory of machine learning, theory of computation, and probability theory. Builds mathematical tools to understand computational and statistical challenges in modern ML, focusing on computational-statistical tradeoffs, phase transition phenomena (All-or-Nothing), connections between statistics and cryptography, and the cost of differential privacy in statistics.
